name: Deino
flavorText: They cannot see, so they tackle and bite to learn about their surroundings. Their bodies are covered in wounds.

name: Sylveon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Magical Ribbon
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Plea-GX
attacks.text: Put 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to them into your opponent's hand. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Find
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Trainer card or a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

attacks.name: Biting Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin, if heads this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Revitalizer
rules: Put 2 Grass Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Geodude

attacks.name: Stone Barrage
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Found in fields and mountains. Mistaking them for boulders, people often step or trip on them.

name: Team Magma Technical Machine 01
rules: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon that has Team Magma in its name. That Pokémon may use this card's attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Team Magma Technical Machine 01.

attacks.name: Crushing Magma
attacks.text: Choose an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon and put that card at the bottom of your opponent's deck.

name: Sliggoo

attacks.name: Gooey
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It drives away opponents by excreting a sticky liquid that can dissolve anything. Its eyes devolved, so it can't see anything.

name: Darkrai-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Pulse
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Darkness Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Head
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Buffer Piece
rules: Attach Buffer Piece to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Damage done to the Pokémon Buffer Piece is attached to by an opponent's attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). At the end of your opponent's turn after you played Buffer Piece, discard Buffer Piece.

name: Glacia's Stadium
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each player's Water Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) has no Weakness.

name: Unfezant

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Strong Winds
attacks.text: Shuffle all cards attached to each player's Pokémon into that player's deck.

flavorText: Males have plumage on their heads. They will never let themselves fell close to anyone other than their Trainers.

name: Full Heal Energy
rules: If you play this card from your hand, the Pokémon you attach it to is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.
Full Heal Energy provides Colorless energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Maintenance
rules: Shuffle 2 cards from your hand into your deck. (If you can't shuffle 2 cards into your deck, you can't play this card.) Then, draw a card.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Umbreon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strafe
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Bullet
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dark Call-GX
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Flygon ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Emerge Charge
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Flygon ex from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your discard pile for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to Flygon ex.

attacks.name: Reactive Blast
attacks.text: You may discard any number of React Energy cards attached to Flygon ex. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage for each Energy card you discarded.

name: Weezing

abilities.name: Detention Gas
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Basic Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Splattering Sludge
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: If one of the twin Koffing inflates, the other one deflates. It constantly mixes its poisonous gases.

name: Hoothoot

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Kick Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It always stands on one foot. Even when attacked, it does not brace itself using both feet.

attacks.name: Water Bullet
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Water Energy attached to Seadra. This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

name: Zweilous

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Since their two heads do not get along and compete with each other for food, they always eat too much.

name: Galarian Cursola

abilities.name: Perish Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, flip a coin. If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy. The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon's core spirit.

name: Growlithe

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon with a loyal nature. It will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.

attacks.name: Play Dead
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Misty's Magikarp.

name: Research Record
rules: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put as many of them as you like back on top of your deck in any order. Then, put the remaining cards on the bottom of your deck in any order.

name: Hydreigon

attacks.name: Cruel Fang
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Dark Burn
attacks.text: Discard as many Darkness Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like. This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Darkness Energy you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It responds to movement by attacking. This scary, three-headed Pokémon devours everything in its path!

name: Dusclops

attacks.name: Disable
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Anyone who dares peer into its body to see its spectral ball of fire will have their spirit stolen away.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Voltage Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Concealing itself in lightning clouds, it flies throughout the Unova region. It creates electricity in its tail.

name: Crustle

abilities.name: Sturdy
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: When its boulder is broken in battles for territory, it feels unsure and begins to weaken.

name: Pokémon Breeder
rules: Draw 2 cards and heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon. If you have no cards in your deck, you can't play this card.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Spiritomb

abilities.name: Sealing Scream
abilities.text: Each player can't play any ACE SPEC cards from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Hexed Mirror
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

abilities.name: Fire Breath
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned. This power can't be used if Houndoom is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Dark Clamp
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Exp. Share
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, you may move 1 basic Energy card that was attached to that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Primal Groudon-EX
rules: Primal Reversion rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes Primal Groudon-EX, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ancientTrait.name: Ω Barrier
ancientTrait.text: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gaia Volcano
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 100 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.

name: Sudowoodo

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Sudowoodo.

flavorText: Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composition appears to be closer to a rock than a plant.

name: Energy Recycle System
rules: Search your discard pile for basic Energy cards. You may either show 1 basic Energy card to your opponent and put it into your hand, or show 3 basic Energy cards to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

attacks.name: Splash About
attacks.text: If Surskit has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Swinub

attacks.name: Generate Cold
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It moves with its sensitive nose pressed to the ground, always searching for food.

name: Jirachi-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Psychic Zone
abilities.text: Don't apply Psychic Weakness when Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) take damage from attacks.

attacks.name: Star Search
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Psychic Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Star Shield-GX
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Probopass

attacks.name: Magnetic Lines
attacks.text: You may move an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heavy Nose
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.

name: Altaria-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bright Tone
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Euphoria-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal all damage from all of your Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Plasma Energy
rules: This card provides Colorless Energy.

name: Marshadow-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Shadow Hunt
abilities.text: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any Basic Pokémon in your discard pile. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

attacks.name: Peerless Hundred Blows-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of basic Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Unown [?]

abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown ? is on your Bench, you may choose a Pokémon in your hand and put it face down. Your opponent guesses a type of that Pokémon. Reveal that card. If your opponent guessed wrong, draw a card. Then, put that card back into your hand.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.text: Discard up to 2 cards from your hand. For each card you discarded, draw a card.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

name: M Sceptile-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ancientTrait.name: θ Stop
ancientTrait.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's Pokémon's Abilities done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jagged Saber
attacks.text: You may attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your hand to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, heal all damage from that Pokémon.

name: Grimer

attacks.name: Nasty Goo
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Minimized
attacks.text: All damage done by attacks to Grimer during your opponent's next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Appears in filthy areas. Thrives by sucking up polluted sludge that is pumped out of factories.

name: Klinklang

attacks.name: Metal Blast
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lock Gear
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

flavorText: The gear with the red core is rotated at high speed for a rapid energy charge.

abilities.name: Bad Sleeping Habits
abilities.text: As long as Snorlax is Asleep, your opponent's Active Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Toss and Turn
attacks.text: If Snorlax is Asleep, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage. (This attack can be used even if Snorlax is Asleep.)

attacks.name: Heavy Press
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage. If tails, Snorlax is now Asleep.

name: Marowak BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Marowak BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Bone Revenge
attacks.text: This attack does 40 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Torchic.

name: Poochyena

attacks.name: Howl in the Dark
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Darkness Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has a very tenacious nature. Its acute sense of smell lets it chase a chosen prey without ever losing track.

name: Energy Root
rules: Attach Energy Root to 1 of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex and Pokémon that has Dark or an owner in its name) that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If the Pokémon Energy Root is attached to is Pokémon-ex or has Dark or an owner in its name, discard Energy Root.
As long as Energy Root is attached to a Pokémon, that Pokémon gets +20 HP and can't use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

name: Aegislash

abilities.name: Stance Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch this Pokémon with an Aegislash in your hand. (Any cards attached to this Pokémon, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Buster Swing
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Generations of kings were attended by these Pokémon, which used their spectral power to manipulate and control people and Pokémon.

name: Nosepass

attacks.name: Pull
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When endangered, it may protect itself by raising its magnetism and drawing iron objects to its body.

name: Magnezone

abilities.name: Magnetic Circuit
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Metal Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin, while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

name: Chikorita

attacks.name: Sleep Scent
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It uses the leaf on its head to determine the temperature and humidity. It loves to sunbathe.

name: Department Store Girl
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon Tool cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Whismur

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the number of your Pokémon that have the Round attack.

flavorText: If it senses danger, it scares the foe by crying out with the volume of a jet-plane engine.

name: Lillipup

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: The long hair around its face provides an amazing radar that lets it sense subtle changes in its surroundings.

name: Goodra

abilities.name: Slip Trip
abilities.text: Each player can't attach any Pokémon Tool cards from his or her hand to any of his or her Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck.

flavorText: This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.

name: Sableye

attacks.name: Limitation
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Supporter cards from their hand during their next turn.

flavorText: It's a fiend for gemstones, so it stalks Carbink. Unfortunately, Gabite almost always grabs them first.

name: Kingdra

attacks.name: Dragon Vortex
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of Water Energy cards and Lightning Energy cards in your discard pile. Then, shuffle all of those cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Tri Bullet
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lives in caves on the seafloor and creates giant whirlpools every time it moves.

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a basic Energy card attached 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Energy Draw
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to Electabuzz. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Reflect Energy
attacks.text: If you have any Benched Pokémon and if there are any basic Energy cards attached to Electabuzz, take 1 of those Energy cards and attach it to 1 of those Pokémon.

name: Relicanth

attacks.name: Grand Swell
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon for each Pokémon Tool and Stadium card your opponent has in play. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: A rare Pokémon discovered during a deep-sea exploration. It has not changed in over 100 million years.

name: Buffer Piece
rules: Attach Buffer Piece to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Damage done to the Pokémon Buffer Piece is attached to by an opponent's attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). At the end of your opponent's turn after you played Buffer Piece, discard Buffer Piece.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: Its upper whiskers are sensors that survey its surroundings. Its lower whiskers are organs that shoot electricity.

name: Pokémon Center
rules: Remove all damage counters from all of your own Pokémon with damage counters on them, then discard all Energy cards attached to those Pokémon.

name: Mewtwo

abilities.name: Pressure
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Active Pokémon's attack do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

name: Warp Point
rules: Your opponent switches 1 of his or her Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. You switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any.

name: Haunter

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its tongue is made of gas. If licked, its victim starts shaking constantly until death eventually comes.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Wurmple and put it on Wurmple. (This counts as evolving Wurmple.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Miracle Essence
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Special Condition. Each Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

attacks.name: Dig Under
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Burrow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done by attacks to Diglett during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

name: Darkrai

attacks.name: Hide in Shadows
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Hole
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: To protect itself, it afflicts those around it with nightmares. However, it means no harm.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Pangoro

attacks.name: Parting Shot
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 60 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Buster Swing
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It charges ahead and bashes its opponents like a berserker, uncaring about any hits it might take. Its arms are mighty enough to snap a telephone pole.

name: Pachirisu

attacks.name: Overshort
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If you discarded a Pokémon Tool card in this way, this attack does 40 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

name: Memory Berry
rules: Attach Memory Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

The Pokémon this card is attached to can use any attack from its Basic Pokémon or its Stage 1 Evolution card. (You still have to pay for that attack's Energy cost.) If that Pokémon attacks, discard this card at the end of the turn.

name: Venonat

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.text: Remove from Venonat the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Poison oozes from all over its body. It catches and eats small bugs at night that are attracted by light.

attacks.name: Confusion Wave
attacks.text: Both Slowpoke and the Defending Pokémon are now Confused.

name: Nidoran ♂

attacks.name: Horn Hazard
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Stiffens its ears to sense danger. The larger, more powerful of its horns secretes venom.

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Ghastly Sound
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play any Supporter cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Finger Poke
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its cheeks hold poison sacs. It tries to catch foes off guard to jab them with toxic fingers.

name: Potion Energy
rules: If you play this card from your hand, remove 1 damage counter from the Pokémon you attach it to, if it has any.
Potion Energy provides Colorless energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Absol

attacks.name: Future Sight
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of either player's deck and put them back in any order.

attacks.name: Doom News
attacks.text: Put all Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.

flavorText: Long ago, superstitions were spread about it, saying it brought disaster. This fed a hatred of it, and it was driven deep into the mountains.

name: Aegislash

abilities.name: Stance Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch this Pokémon with an Aegislash in your hand. (Any cards attached to this Pokémon, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.)

attacks.name: King's Shield
attacks.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn. This Pokémon can't use King's Shield during your next turn.

flavorText: Generations of kings were attended by these Pokémon, which used their spectral power to manipulate and control people and Pokémon.

name: Pokémon Fan Club
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Unfezant

ancientTrait.name: Δ Evolution
ancientTrait.text: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Feather Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, each of this Pokémon's attacks does 80 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Sky Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Males have plumage on their heads. They will never let themselves feel close to anyone other than their Trainers.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever 30 or less damage is done to Onix (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: As it grows, the stone portions of its body harden to become similar to a diamond, though colored black.

name: Gengar

abilities.name: Life Shaker
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Psychic Pokémon to another of your Psychic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: On the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be Gengar's doing.

abilities.name: Freefloating
abilities.text: If Tentacool has no Energy attached to it, Tentacool's Retreat Cost is 0.

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Bubbles
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, you can't use this attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Totodile

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Totodile but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavorText: Its highly developed jaws are powerful enough to crush anything. Even experienced adult trainers must handle it with great caution.

name: Piplup

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

name: Alolan Rattata

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Bite attack's base damage is 60.

flavorText: With its incisors, it gnaws through doors and infiltrates people's homes. Then, with a twitch of its whiskers, it steals whatever food it finds.

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

name: Dialga

attacks.name: Time Freeze
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

name: Herdier

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

flavorText: It loyally follows its Trainer's orders. For ages, they have helped Trainers raise Pokémon.

name: Entei-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Brave Burn-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 150 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Seaking.

attacks.name: Undulate
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Seaking.

name: Energy Restore
rules: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, put a basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand. If you don't have that many basic Energy cards in your discard pile, put all of them into your hand.

name: Mew ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Versatile
abilities.text: Mew ex can use the attacks of all Pokémon in play as its own. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

attacks.name: Power Move
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to Mew ex. Shuffle your deck afterward. Then, you may switch Mew ex with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Hippopotas
flavorText: It enshrouds itself with sand to protect itself from germs. It does not enjoy getting wet.

name: Noivern-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Distort
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Item cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Sonic Volume
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Special Energy cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Boomburst-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 1 or Stage 2 card that evolves from that Pokémon in your hand, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.)

name: Red Card
rules: Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 4 cards.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Hypnotic Stare
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Mienfoo

attacks.name: Steady Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attacks. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies.

name: Luvdisc

attacks.name: Rendezvous
attacks.text: Reveal the top card of your deck and put it into your hand. If that card is a Pokémon, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Synchro Dance
attacks.text: If Luvdisc and the Defending Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in warm seas. It is said that a couple finding this Pokémon will be blessed with eternal love.

name: Conkeldurr

attacks.name: Facade
attacks.text: If this Pokémon is Burned or Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Drain Punch
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Rather than rely on force, they master moves that utilize the centrifugal force of spinning concrete.

name: Mewtwo

abilities.name: Mind Report
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile on top of your deck.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It was created by a scientist after years of horrific gene-splicing and DNA-engineering experiments.

name: High Pressure System
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Each player pays Colorless less to retreat his or her Fire and Water Pokémon.

abilities.name: Spray Splash
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This power can't be used if Kingdra is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Dragon Steam
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Fire Pokémon in play, this attack's base damage is 20 instead of 60.

name: Sage's Training
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose any 2 cards you find there and put them into your hand. Discard the other cards.

name: Milcery

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

name: Rhydon

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Standing on its hind legs freed its forelegs and made it smarter. It is very forgetful, however.

name: Champions Festival
rules: Once during each player's turn, if that player has 6 Pokémon in play, they may heal 10 damage from each of their Pokémon.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Volbeat

attacks.name: Illumisile
attacks.text: If you don't have Illumise in play, this attack does nothing. Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Firefly Light
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

flavorText: It emits light from its tail to communicate. It loves the sweet aroma given off by Illumise.

name: Misty's Determination
rules: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, look at the top 8 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Hydreigon BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Hydreigon BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Calamity Blast
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Flaaffy does 20 damage to itself.

name: Klinklang

attacks.name: Charge Beam
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: The gear with the red core is rotated at high speed for a rapid energy charge.

name: Morelull

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull's head. The caps regrow overnight.

name: G Booster
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Genesect-EX this card is attached to can also use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

attacks.name: G Booster
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Copycat
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, count the number of cards in your opponent's hand and draw that many cards.

name: Clefable

attacks.name: Follow Me
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Moonblast
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its hearing is so acute it can hear a pin drop over half a mile away. It lives on quiet mountains.

name: Latias-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Bright Down
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent's Pokémon with Abilities.

attacks.name: Barrier Break
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Machop
flavorText: It hefts a GRAVELER repeatedly to strengthen its entire body. It uses every type of martial arts.

name: Shieldon

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon lived in primeval jungles. Few enemies would have been willing to square off against its heavily armored face, so it's thought.

name: Hydreigon

abilities.name: Dark Aura
abilities.text: All Energy attached to this Pokémon are Darkness Energy instead of their usual type.

attacks.name: Berserker Blade
attacks.text: Does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

abilities.name: Voltage Increase
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Lightning Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to Raichu. This power can't be used if Raichu is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Mega Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to Raichu.

name: Machop
flavorText: It's said that not even pro wrestlers can take down a Machop.

name: Swinub

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: If it smells something enticing, it dashes off headlong to find the source of the aroma.

name: Durant

attacks.name: Pull Out
attacks.text: Put a card from your discard pile on top of your deck.

attacks.name: Iron Head
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Durant dig nests in mountains. They build their complicated, interconnected tunnels into mazes.

name: Alolan Rattata

attacks.name: Call for the Boss
attacks.text: Search your deck for Alolan Raticate or Alolan Raticate-GX, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When the sun goes down, it becomes active. It runs around town on a chase for good food for the boss of its nest—Raticate.

name: Sigilyph

abilities.name: Counterattack
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic Assault
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians' emissaries.

abilities.name: Self-control
abilities.text: Zubat can't be Paralyzed.

name: Pokémon Flute
rules: Choose 1 Basic Pokémon card from your opponent's discard pile and put it onto his or her Bench. (You can't play Pokémon Flute if your opponent's Bench is full.)

name: Jangmo-o
flavorText: It expresses its feelings by smacking its scales. Metallic sounds echo through the tall mountains where Jangmo-o lives.

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Giratina

attacks.name: Hex
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Shadow Claw
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.

name: Dusknoir

abilities.name: Grim Marking
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Psych Up
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Psych Up attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It is said to take lost spirits into its pliant body and guide them home.

name: Spheal

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It rolls across ice floes to reach shore because its body is poorly shaped for swimming.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Aqua Energy
rules: Aqua Energy can be attached only to a Pokémon with Team Aqua in its name. Aqua Energy provides Water and Darkness Energy but provides 2 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play and has no other effect than providing Energy.) At the end of your turn, discard Aqua Energy.

name: Pawniard
flavorText: They fight at Bisharp's command. They cling to their prey and inflict damage by sinking their blades into it.

name: Loudred

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: The shock waves from its cries can tip over trucks. It stamps its feet to power up.

name: Escavalier

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: These Pokémon evolve by wearing the shell covering of a Shelmet. The steel armor protects their whole body.

name: Balloon Berry
rules: Attach Balloon Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
As long as Balloon Berry is attached to a Pokémon, that Pokémon's Retreat Cost is 0. When this Pokémon retreats, discard Balloon Berry.

name: Holon Legacy
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each Pokémon in play that has δ on its card (both yours and your opponent's) has no Weakness and can't use any Poké-Powers.

name: Fluffy Berry
rules: Attach Fluffy Berry to 1 of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex and Pokémon that has Dark or an owner in its name) that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If the Pokémon Fluffy Berry is attached to is Pokémon-ex or has Dark or an owner in its name, discard Fluffy Berry.
As long as Fluffy Berry is attached to a Pokémon, that Pokémon's Retreat Cost is 0.

name: Darkrai-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Cloak
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Darkness Energy attached to it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Night Spear
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Alolan Raticate

attacks.name: Enhanced Fang
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It forms a group of Rattata, which it assumes command of. Each group has its own territory, and disputes over food happen often.

name: Altaria

abilities.name: Fight Song
abilities.text: Your Dragon Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: If it bonds with a person, it will gently envelop the friend with its soft wings, then hum.

name: Metagross

abilities.name: Plasma Search
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Team Plasma card, reveal it, and put it in your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. You may not use an Ability with the same name during your turn.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: With four linked brains, it's more intelligent than a supercomputer, and it uses calculations to analyze foes.

attacks.name: Dark Streak
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Mew

abilities.name: Bench Barrier
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by your opponent's attacks.

attacks.name: Psypower
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

name: Hoppip

abilities.name: Cottonweed
abilities.text: If Hoppip has any Grass Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost for Hoppip is 0.

attacks.name: Hover Heal
attacks.text: Remove all Special Conditions from Hoppip.

flavorText: It drifts on winds. It is said that when HOPPIP gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.

name: Pokédex
rules: Look at up to 5 cards from the top of your deck and rearrange them as you like.

name: Alolan Raticate-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Chuck Away
attacks.text: Discard up to 2 cards from your hand. This attack does 40 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Item Maniac-GX
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 6 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Drifloon
flavorText: Its round body is stuffed with souls and expands each time it leads someone away.

name: Great Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Heavy Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon with a Retreat Cost of 3 or more, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Dark Raichu

attacks.name: Surprise Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, flip another coin. If the second coin is heads, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If the second coin is tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Stores up electricity in its body, then suddenly releases it to surprise and shock everyone.

attacks.name: Growth Spurt
attacks.text: Attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to Shroomish.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Holon Adventurer
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Discard a card from your hand. If you can't discard a card from your hand, you can't play this card.
Draw 3 cards. If you discarded a Pokémon that has δ on its card, draw 4 cards instead.

attacks.name: Sleepy Song
attacks.text: Each Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Scizor-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Gale Thrust
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

name: Wobbuffet V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gritty Comeback
attacks.text: Switch all damage counters on this Pokémon with those on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Bind
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

name: Hakamo-o

attacks.name: Noble Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It leaps at its prey with a courageous shout. Its scaly punches tear its opponents to shreds.

name: Expert Belt
rules: Attach Expert Belt to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

The Pokémon this card is attached to gets +20 HP and that Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). When the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 1 more Prize card.

name: Stoutland

attacks.name: Odor Sleuth
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: This extremely wise Pokémon excels at rescuing people stranded at sea or in the mountains.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Heartfelt Song
attacks.text: Discard a Darkness Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It captivates foes with its huge, round eyes, then lulls them to sleep by singing a soothing melody.

name: Voltorb

attacks.name: Magnetic Bomb
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage. If tails, Voltorb does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

name: Sunkern

attacks.name: Growth
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, you may attach up to 2 Grass Energy cards from your hand to Sunkern.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Remove a number of damage counters from Sunkern equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Sunkern has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavorText: It may drop out of the sky suddenly. If attacked by a Spearow, it violently shakes its leaves.

name: Low Pressure System
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Each Grass and Lightning Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets +10 HP.

name: Roserade

attacks.name: Poison Seed
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Each of its hands contains different toxins, but both hands can jab with near-fatal power.

name: Pangoro

attacks.name: Sky Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It boasts superb physical strength. Those who wish to become Pangoro's Trainer have no choice but to converse with their fists.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Bisharp pursues prey in the company of a large group of Pawniard. Then Bisharp finishes off the prey.

name: Sylveon

attacks.name: Curly Ribbon
attacks.text: Move an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It sends a soothing aura from its ribbonlike feelers to calm fights.

name: Beldum

abilities.name: Metal Chain
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you attach a Metal Energy card from your hand to Beldum (excluding effects of attacks or Poké-Powers), you may search your deck for Beldum and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Beldum is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Beldum does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It converses with others by using magnetic pulses. In a swarm, they move in perfect unison.

name: Staryu

attacks.name: Cosmic Draw
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Evolved Pokémon in play, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: If its body is torn, it can grow back if the red core remains. The core flashes at midnight.

name: Stunky

attacks.name: Gnaw and Run
attacks.text: Switch Stunky with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It protects itself by spraying a noxious fluid from its rear. The stench lingers for 24 hours.

name: Magma Energy
rules: Magma Energy can be attached only to a Pokémon with Team Magma in its name. Magma Energy provides Fighting and /or Darkness Energy but provides 2 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play and has no other effect than providing Energy.) At the end of your turn, discard Magma Energy.

name: Latias

attacks.name: Eon Connection
attacks.text: Draw a card. If Latios is on your Bench, draw 1 more card.

flavorText: It can telepathically communicate with people. It changes its appearance using its down that refracts light.

name: Makuhita

attacks.name: Arm Thrust
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It toughens its body by slamming into thick trees. Many snapped trees can be found near its nest.

name: Alcremie

abilities.name: Sharing Sweets
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may have each player draw a card.

attacks.name: Wonder Shine
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it's decorated with cream.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magnemite does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: Uses anti-gravity to stay suspended. Appears without warning and uses attacks like Thunder Wave.

name: Protection Cube
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Prevent all damage done to the Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks it uses.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Cyndaquil
flavorText: It has a timid nature. If it is startled, the flames on its back burn more vigorously.

name: Rotom

attacks.name: Cycle Draw
attacks.text: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.text: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Research continues on this Pokémon, which could be the power source of a unique motor.

abilities.name: Prismatic Body
abilities.text: Each basic Energy card attached to Ditto provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.

attacks.name: Copy
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Copy copies that attack. This attack does nothing if Ditto doesn't have the Energy necessary to use that attack. (You must still do anything else required in order to use that attack.)

name: Shauna
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: ESP
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If exactly 1 is heads, draw a card. If exactly 2 are heads, this attack does 20 damage. If all 3 are heads, choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. Misty's Psyduck copies that attack except for its Energy costs. (No matter what type the Defending Pokémon is, Misty's Psyduck's type is still Water.)

abilities.name: Red Armor
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to Scizor by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have any Special Energy cards attached to them.

attacks.name: Metal Scissors
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Metal Energy attached to Scizor.

name: Walrein ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Icy Aura
abilities.text: As long as Walrein ex is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each Active Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) between turns, excluding Water Pokémon.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Ice Throw
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Fighting Pokémon, this attack's base damage is 120 instead of 80.

name: Blitzle
flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

name: Hippowdon

attacks.name: Resistance Desert
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by Pokémon-EX.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It blasts internally stored sand from ports on its body to create a towering twister for attack.

name: Latios-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mach Flight
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Luster Purge
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Foul Odor
attacks.text: Both the Defending Pokémon and Gloom are now Confused (after doing damage).

name: G Scope
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Genesect-EX this card is attached to can also use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

attacks.name: G Scope
attacks.text: This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Fisherman
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your discard pile for 4 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.

name: Cobalion

attacks.name: Metal Horns
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sacred Sword
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Sacred Sword during your next turn.

flavorText: This legendary Pokémon battled against humans to protect Pokémon. Its personality is calm and composed.

name: Paras

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Burrows to suck tree roots. The mushrooms on its back grow by drawing nutrients from the bug host.

name: Mienfoo
flavorText: It takes pride in the speed at which it can use moves. What it loses in power, it makes up for in quantity.

name: Pangoro

attacks.name: Untamed Punch
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 more damage, and both Active Pokémon are now Confused.

attacks.name: Double Stomp
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: From the slight twitches of its bamboo leaf, it deduces its opponent's movements. It's eager to tussle but kindhearted toward its companions.

name: Piloswine

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gathering Footsteps
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Colorless in the Retreat Cost of your Swinub, Piloswine, and Mamoswine.

flavorText: Because the long hair all over its body obscures its sight, it just keeps charging repeatedly.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Metal Sound
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Resonance
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: The units at its sides generate electromagnetic waves that keep it airborne. It feeds on electricity.

name: Carvanha

attacks.name: Gnaw Through
attacks.text: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: If they scent the faintest trace of blood, they rush to attack en masse. When alone, they're rather cowardly.

name: Sandygast

attacks.name: Sneaky Placement
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: If you build sand mounds when you're playing, destroy them before you go home, or they may get possessed and become Sandygast.

name: Fletchling

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Despite the beauty of its lilting voice, it's merciless to intruders that enter its territory.

attacks.name: Sleepy Ball
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Shining Celebi

abilities.name: Time Recall
abilities.text: Each of your evolved Pokémon can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

flavorText: This Pokémon wanders across time. Grass and trees flourish in the forests in which it has appeared.

name: Kyurem-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Glaciate
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Icecalibur
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Stall
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only on your first turn. Discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

abilities.name: Lightweight
abilities.text: You pay Colorless less to retreat Skiploom for each Grass Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Break Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage. If tails, this attack does 10 damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Silvally-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Gyro Unit
abilities.text: Your Basic Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Turbo Drive
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rebel-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Pidgeot Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Pidgeot-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Frostbite
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Mirage
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Munna

attacks.name: Moonlight
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It eats dreams and releases mist. The mist is pink when it's eating a good dream, and black when it's eating a nightmare.

name: Pidgey

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

flavorText: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

name: Pidove

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Each follows its Trainer's orders as best it can, but they sometimes fail to understand complicated commands.

name: Alolan Grimer

attacks.name: Division
attacks.text: Search your deck for Alolan Grimer and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Slippery Sludge
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A Grimer, which had been brought in to solve a problem with garbage, developed over time into this form.

name: Latios

attacks.name: Sky Blade
attacks.text: If Latias is on your Bench, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.

name: Electrode

attacks.name: Electribeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Self Destruct
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 100 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is known to drift on winds if it is bloated to bursting with stored electricity.

name: Master Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

name: Drifblim

abilities.name: Tag Transport
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch your Active TAG TEAM Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: There's a rumor that if you catch a Drifblim floating on the wind at dusk, you'll be carried away to the afterlife.

name: Team Flare Grunt
rules: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Rayquaza-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Celestial Roar
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Burst
attacks.text: Discard all basic Fire Energy or all basic Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage times the number of Energy you discarded.

name: Level Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon with 90 HP or less, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Woobat

attacks.name: Nasal Suction
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Air Cutter
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings.

name: Exploud

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Cacophony
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, discard the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it.

flavorText: Its roar in battle shakes the ground like a tremor–or like an earthquake has struck.

name: Mysterious Shard
rules: Attach Mysterious Shard to 1 of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If the Pokémon Mysterious Shard is attached to is a Pokémon-ex, discard this card.
Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to the Pokémon that Mysterious Shard is attached to by your opponent's Pokémon-ex. Discard this card at the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Clamp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

name: Mary's Request
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw a card. If you don't have any Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon in play, draw 2 more cards.

name: Crystal Shard
rules: Attach Crystal Shard to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, that Pokémon's type is Colorless. If that Pokémon attacks, discard this card at the end of the turn.

name: Drampa-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Righteous Edge
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Berserk
attacks.text: If your Benched Pokémon have any damage counters on them, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Big Wheel-GX
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 10 cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Fossil Excavator
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your deck or discard pile for a Trainer card that has Fossil in its name or a Stage 1 or Stage 2 Evolution card that evolves from a Fossil. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. If you searched your deck, shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Double Rainbow Energy
rules: Double Rainbow Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex). While in play, Double Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides 2 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy when not in play and has no effect other than providing Energy.) Damage done to your opponent's Pokémon by the Pokémon Double Rainbow Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). When the Pokémon Double Rainbow Energy is attached to is no longer an Evolved Pokémon, discard Double Rainbow Energy.

name: Ho-Oh-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sacred Fire
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Phoenix Burn
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Phoenix Burn during your next turn.

attacks.name: Eternal Light-GX
attacks.text: Put 3 in any combination of Fire Pokémon-GX or Fire Pokémon-EX from your discard pile onto your Bench. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Chansey

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A kindly Pokémon that lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

name: Mr. Briney's Compassion
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play (excluding Pokémon-ex). Return that Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

name: Holon Mentor
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Discard a card from your hand. If you can't discard a card from your hand, you can't play this card.
Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon that each has 100 HP or less, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Sacred Ash
rules: Shuffle 5 Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Lucky Egg
rules: Attach Lucky Egg to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
When the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.

name: Rotom

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy attached to Rotom. Flip a coin for each Energy card you discarded. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Uproar
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its electric-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

name: Copycat
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, count the number of cards in your opponent's hand and draw that many cards.

name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Ominous Eyes
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: What gives meaning to its life is surprising others. If you set your ear against the red orbs around its neck, you can hear shrieking.

name: Sneasel

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Beat Up
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Pokémon you have in play. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It uses its claws to poke holes in eggs so it can slurp out the insides. Breeders consider it a scourge and will drive it away or eradicate it.

name: Scott
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your deck for up to 3 cards in any combination of Supporter cards and Stadium cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Regice ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

attacks.name: Ice Barrier
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Regice Star by your opponent's Pokémon-ex during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Final Blizzard
attacks.text: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left and Regice Star is the only Pokémon you have in play, this attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Persian

attacks.name: Raid
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved from Meowth during this turn, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its lithe muscles allow it to walk without making a sound. It attacks in an instant.

name: Trapinch

abilities.name: Gather Sand
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Trapinch is your Active Pokémon, you may search your discard pile for a basic Fighting card and attach it to Trapinch.
