attacks.name: Grind
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Trapinch.

flavorText: It makes a conical pit in desert sand and lies in wait at the bottom for prey to come tumbling down.

name: Rattata

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It searches for food all day. It gnaws on hard objects to wear down its fangs, which grow constantly during its lifetime.

attacks.name: Energy Catch
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Skitty does 10 damage to itself.

name: Raichu-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Powerful Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Lightning Energy attached to your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Voltail-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage for each damage counter on Totodile.

name: Darkness Energy
rules: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this Effect if the Pokémon that Darkness Energy is attached to isn't Darkness. Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Palossand

attacks.name: Sand Sink
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck. If this Pokémon has a Cursed Shovel attached, discard 2 more cards from the top of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Super Absorption
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Once it has whipped up a sandstorm to halt its opponents in their tracks, this terrifying Pokémon snatches away their vitality.

name: Master Ball
rules: Look at the top 7 cards from your deck. Choose a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from those cards, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Put the other 6 cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Zorua

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

name: Pawniard
flavorText: Blades comprise this Pokémon's entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It captivates foes with its huge, round eyes, then lulls them to sleep by singing a soothing melody.

name: Drilbur

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: By spinning its body, it can dig straight through the ground at a speed of 30 mph.

name: Beldum

attacks.name: Core Beam
attacks.text: Discard a Metal Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its cells are all magnets. It uses magnetism to communicate with others of its kind.

name: Bastiodon

abilities.name: Earthen Shield
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Metal Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon that have any Special Energy attached to them.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: You may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It lived in the same environments as Rampardos. Their fossils have been found together—seemingly from after they'd fought to the finish.

name: Poochyena

attacks.name: Howl
attacks.text: Search your deck for Poochyena and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: A Pokémon with persistent nature, it chases its prey until the prey becomes exhausted.

name: Doduo

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Accelerating Stab
attacks.text: Doduo can't use Accelerating Stab during your next turn.

flavorText: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

abilities.name: Underwater Dive
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Lanturn's type is Water until the end of your turn. This power can't be used if Lanturn is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Powerful Spark
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Zebstrika

abilities.name: Sprint
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard your hand and draw 4 cards.

flavorText: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

name: Yanma

abilities.name: Free Flight
abilities.text: If Yanma has no Energy attached to it, Yanma's Retreat Cost is 0.

flavorText: Its large eyes can scan 360 degrees. It looks in all directions to seek out insects as its prey.

name: Mamoswine

abilities.name: Thick Fat
abilities.text: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Fire or Water Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Primordial Boom
attacks.text: If you have a Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 more damage. If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its impressive tusks are made of ice. The population thinned when it turned warm after the ice age.

name: Yveltal-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Absorb Vitality
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sonic Evil
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Doom Count-GX
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has exactly 4 damage counters on it, that Pokémon is Knocked Out. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Shiinotic

attacks.name: Flickering Light
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Fear the Forest
attacks.text: If Glimwood Tangle is in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

name: Mankey

attacks.name: Mischief
attacks.text: Shuffle your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Anger
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: Normally friendly, it quickly becomes angry if it doesn't get what it wants.

name: Spearow

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Very protective of its territory, it flaps its short wings busily to dart around at high speed.

name: Mienshao

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Double Whip
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Using the long fur on its arms like whips, it launches into combo attacks that, once started, no one can stop.

name: Purrloin

attacks.name: Tail Trickery
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its cute act is a ruse. When victims let down their guard, they find their items taken. It attacks with sharp claws.

name: Swinub

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food. It sometimes discovers hot springs.

name: Lickitung

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: Instead of hands, it uses its tongue, which is twice its height. Its sticky saliva grips anything.

name: Unown [L]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Laugh]
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, each player shuffles his or her deck. This power can be used even if Unown [L] is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is believed that the variety of types of this Pokémon were created by evolutionary adaptation, as each possesses a different ability.

name: Beldum

attacks.name: Allure
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It converses with others by using magnetic pulses. In a swarm, they move in perfect unison.

name: Full Heal
rules: Remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Burrow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done by attacks to Dugtrio during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Dig Under
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness or Resistance. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

name: M Scizor-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Iron Crusher
attacks.text: You may discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon or a Stadium card in play.

name: Mightyena

attacks.name: Dark Fang
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It chases down prey in a pack of around ten. They defeat foes with perfectly coordinated teamwork.

name: Ampharos-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunder Rod
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and attach as many Lightning Energy cards you find there as you like to this Pokémon. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Sparkling Tail
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Klefki

abilities.name: Secret Key
abilities.text: Each of your Fairy Pokémon's Resistance is now -40.

attacks.name: Fairy Lock
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: These key collectors threaten any attackers by fiercely jingling their keys at them.

name: Smoochum
rules: If your Active Pokémon is a Baby Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.

attacks.name: Energy Kiss
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Professor Oak
rules: Discard your hand, then draw 7 cards.

name: Sharpedo

abilities.name: Rough Skin
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aqua Impact
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It has a sad history. In the past, its dorsal fin was a treasured foodstuff, so this Pokémon became a victim of overfishing.

name: Zacian

attacks.name: Metal Armament
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Amazing Sword
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Pokémon VMAX in play, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: Now armed with a weapon it used in ancient times, this Pokémon needs only a single strike to fell even Gigantamax Pokémon.

name: Kommo-o

attacks.name: War Cry
attacks.text: If you have fewer Pokémon in play than your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Clanging Scales
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When it spots enemies, it threatens them by jingling the scales on its tail. Weak opponents will crack and flee in panic.

name: Patrat

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Extremely cautious, they take shifts to maintain a constant watch of their nest. They feel insecure without a lookout.

name: Mankey

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Mankey.

flavorText: It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

name: Pokédex
rules: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.text: After your attack, you may switch Squirtle with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Swinub

attacks.name: Freezing Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Swinub does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It loves eating mushrooms that grow under dead grass. It also finds hot springs while foraging.

name: Watchog

attacks.name: Watcheck
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back on top of his or her deck in any order.

attacks.name: Quick Tail Smash
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

attacks.name: Psycho Waves
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card attached to Venonat. The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

name: TV Reporter
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw 3 cards. Then discard any 1 card from your hand.

name: Alolan Muk-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Chemical Breath
attacks.text: This attack does 70 more damage for each Special Condition affecting your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tri Hazard-GX
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Burned, Paralyzed, and Poisoned. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Holon Fossil
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for an Omanyte, Kabuto, Aerodactyl, Aerodactyl ex, Lileep, or Anorith and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. If tails, put an Omanyte, Kabuto, Aerodactyl, Aerodactyl ex, Lileep, or Anorith from your hand onto your Bench. Treat the new Benched Pokémon as a Basic Pokémon.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.

name: Gible
flavorText: It attacks using its huge mouth. While its attacks are powerful, it hurts itself out of clumsiness, too.

name: Energy Charge
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for 2 Energy cards (1 if there is only 1), show them to your opponent, and shuffle them into your deck.

attacks.name: Lazy Punch
attacks.text: Slakoth can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Recharge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Voltorb. Shuffle your deck afterward.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.text: As long as Turtwig GL's remaining HP is 60 or less, each of Turtwig GL's attacks does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: After your attack, remove from Turtwig GL the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Wooper
flavorText: This Pokémon lives in cold water. It will leave the water to search for food when it gets cold outside.

name: Unown [S]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Search]
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at 1 of your Prize cards. Return that Prize card face down. This power can be used even if Unown [S] is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is believed that the variety of types of this Pokémon were created by evolutionary adaptation, as each possesses a different ability.

attacks.name: Poison Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Landorus-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hammerhead
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Land's Judgment
attacks.text: You may discard all Fighting Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.

name: Team Flare Grunt
rules: Discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Fearow

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: With its huge and magnificent wings, it can keep aloft without ever having to land or rest.

name: Pokémon Center
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may heal 20 damage from 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.text: Don't apply Resistance.

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon (1 if he or she has only 1). This attack does 10 damage to each of those Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Hatenna

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures' emotions. If you don't have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Energy Stream
attacks.text: Attach a Metal Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Scissors
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It leads a group of Pawniard. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses.

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Moomoo Milk
rules: Choose 1 of your Pokémon. Flip 2 coins. For each heads, remove 3 damage counters from that Pokémon.

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

abilities.name: Opponent's Choice
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may reveal the top 2 cards of your deck and your opponent chooses 1 of them. Put that card into your hand and the other card on the bottom of your deck. This power can't be used if Slowking is affected by a Special Condition.

name: Mewtwo-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Full Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Super Absorption
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psystrike-GX
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Regirock ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

attacks.name: Dig Drain
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Regirock Star.

attacks.name: Final Blast
attacks.text: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left and Regirock Star is the only Pokémon you have in play, this attack's base damage is 100 instead of 30.

name: Chansey

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A kindly Pokémon that lays highly nutritious eggs and shares them with injured Pokémon or people.

name: Skorupi

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.

name: Mawile

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Attached to its head is a huge set of jaws formed by horns. It can chew through iron beams.

name: Nidoran ♀

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Nidoran ♀ or Nidoran ♂ and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

flavorText: Although small, its venomous barbs make this Pokémon dangerous. The female has smaller horns.

name: Musharna

attacks.name: Sleepy Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. During Pokémon Checkup, your opponent flips 2 coins instead of 1. If either of them is tails, that Pokémon is still Asleep.

attacks.name: Super Hypnoblast
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: It drowses and dreams all the time. It's best to leave it be if it's just woken up, as it's a terrible grump when freshly roused from sleep.

name: Purrloin

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

attacks.name: Fasten Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Their cute act is a ruse. They trick people and steal their valuables just to see the looks on their faces.

name: Miltank

abilities.name: Moomoo Malt
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, heal 90 damage from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sitdown Splash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its milk is high in calories and packed with nutrients. Therefore, if you drink too much of it, you may wind up with a body like Miltank's.

name: Aether Foundation Employee
rules: Put 3 Pokémon that have "Alolan" in their names from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Guzzlord

attacks.name: Lord's Valley
attacks.text: If you have exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, discard the top 10 cards of your deck.

flavorText: A dangerous Ultra Beast, it appears to be eating constantly, but for some reason its droppings have never been found.

name: Tyranitar

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Trample
attacks.text: For each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's), flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: If it rampages, it knocks down mountains and buries rivers. Maps must be redrawn afterward.

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Tranquill

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It can return to its Trainer's location regardless of the distance separating them.

name: Revive
rules: Put 1 Basic Pokémon card from your discard pile onto your Bench. Put damage counters on that Pokémon equal to half its HP (rounded down to the nearest 10). (You can't play Revive if your Bench is full.)

name: Pokémon Retriever
rules: Search your discard pile for Basic Pokémon and Evolution cards. You may either show 1 Basic Pokémon or Evolution card to your opponent and put it into your hand, or show a combination of 3 Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

name: Island Hermit
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Choose up to 2 of your Prize cards and put them face up. (These cards remain face up for the rest of the game.) Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Gooey Thread
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Mudkip

attacks.name: Endeavor
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

name: Potion
rules: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Ingrain
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to Tangela. Shuffle your deck afterward. If you do, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Tangela during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Tickle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is shrouded by blue vines. No one has seen the face hidden behind this growth of vines.

name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Brutal Bash
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of Darkness Pokémon you have in play.

attacks.name: Dark Rush
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

name: Gallade Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Gallade-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Altaria-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Powerful Gain
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 60 more damage and heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shining Wind
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.

name: Metang

attacks.name: Core Beam
attacks.text: Discard a Metal Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When two Beldum link together, their psychic power is doubled. Their intelligence, however, remains unchanged.

name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Lightning Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.

name: M Ampharos-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Exavolt
attacks.text: You may do 50 more damage and leave your opponent's Active Pokémon Paralyzed. If you do, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

name: Scoop Up Cyclone
rules: Put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

name: Startling Megaphone
rules: Discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Hippopotas

attacks.name: Nose Jet
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It enshrouds itself with sand to protect itself from germs. It does not enjoy getting wet.

name: Dragonite-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Bust In
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may move any number of basic Energy attached to your Pokémon to this Pokémon. If you do, switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jet Sonic
attacks.text: You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage.

name: Black Belt
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

You may use this card only if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent. During this turn, each of your Active Pokémon's attacks does 40 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Star Boomerang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, return Misty's Staryu and all cards attached to it to your hand. (Either way, this attack does its damage.)

name: Old Amber
rules: Play Old Amber as if it were a Colorless Basic Pokémon. (Old Amber counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Old Amber is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Old Amber can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Old Amber from play. (This doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Hard Amber
abilities.text: As long as Old Amber is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to Old Amber by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

name: Raticate

attacks.name: Transfer Junk
attacks.text: Put a Team Plasma Pokémon, a Team Plasma Trainer card, and a Team Plasma Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: With its long fangs, this surprisingly violent Pokémon can gnaw away even thick concrete with ease.

name: Absol

abilities.name: Dark Ambition
abilities.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, its Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

attacks.name: Shadow Seeker
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: Although it's said to bring disaster, in actuality, this Pokémon possesses a calm disposition and warns people of any crises that loom.

abilities.name: Slippery Skin
abilities.text: As long as the Defending Pokémon is an Evolved Pokémon, Dunsparce's Retreat Cost is 0.

attacks.name: Tail Shake
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Diggersby

attacks.name: Ear Dig
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: As powerful as an excavator, its ears can reduce dense bedrock to rubble. When it's finished digging, it lounges lazily.

name: Lotad

attacks.name: Water Sport
attacks.text: If Lotad has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It looks like an aquatic plant and serves as a ferry to Pokémon that can't swim.

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

attacks.name: Silver Feather
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, when your opponent puts a Basic Pokémon from his or her hand onto his or her Bench, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Metal Max
attacks.text: Discard all Metal Energy attached to Skarmory FB. Flip a coin for each Energy card you discarded. This attack does 20 damage plus 40 damage for each heads.

name: Sandile

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

name: Wally's Training
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your deck for a card that evolves from your Active Pokémon (choose 1 if there are 2) and put it on your Active Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Blaziken ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blaze Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage. If tails, this attack does 30 damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Volcanic Ash
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to Blaziken ex and then choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Perfect Metal
abilities.text: Steelix can't be affected by any Special Conditions

attacks.name: Energy Stream
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Steelix.

attacks.name: Gaia Crush
attacks.text: You may discard any Stadium card in play.

name: Totodile

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Totodile.

flavorText: It is small but rough and tough. It won't hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

name: Swoobat

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Charming Stamp
attacks.text: Your opponent chooses 1 of their own Pokémon. This attack does 90 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It shakes its tail vigorously when it emits ultrasonic waves strong enough to reduce concrete to rubble.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Pikachu does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can cause lightning storms.

name: Bellsprout

attacks.name: Vine Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't use any Poké-Powers during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Careless Tackle
attacks.text: Bellsprout does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It prefers hot and humid environments. It is quick at capturing prey with its vines.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Headache
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play any Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Latent Power
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does no damage to the Defending Pokémon. Instead, Psyduck is now Confused.

flavorText: Overwhelmed by enigmatic abilities, it suffers a constant headache. It sometimes uses mysterious powers.

name: Mom's Kindness
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw 2 cards.

name: Electrike

attacks.name: Electromagnetic Jam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't attach any Energy cards from his or her hand to the Active Pokémon during his or her next turn.

flavorText: Using electricity stored in its fur, it stimulates its muscles to heighten its reaction speed.

name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Gather Energy
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Hocus Pinkus
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Their fur feels so good that if two of them snuggle together, they won't want to be separated.

name: Mismagius

abilities.name: Mysterious Message
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand. If you drew any cards in this way, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Feared for its wrath and the curses it spreads, this Pokémon will also, on a whim, cast spells that help people.

name: Metal Energy
rules: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn't Metal. Metal Energy provides Metal Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

attacks.name: Synchronize
attacks.text: This attack can't be used unless Sabrina's Abra and the Defending Pokémon have the same number of Energy cards attached to them.

name: Phoebe's Stadium
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each player pays ColorlessColorless less to retreat his or her Psychic Pokémon.

name: Magmar

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Born in the spout of a volcano, its body is covered by flames that shimmer like the sun.

name: Old Amber
rules: Play Old Amber as if it were a Colorless Basic Pokémon. (Old Amber counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Old Amber is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Old Amber can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Old Amber from play. (This doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Hard Amber
abilities.text: As long as Old Amber is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to Old Amber by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

name: Chansey

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lucky Punch
attacks.text: If you don't have exactly 7 cards in your hand, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It is said to deliver happiness. Being compassionate, it shares its egg with injured people.

name: Healing Scarf
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to the Pokémon this card is attached to, heal 20 damage from it.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Uxie

abilities.name: Secret Territory
abilities.text: If you have Mesprit and Azelf in play, apply Weakness for each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) as ×4 instead.

flavorText: Known as "The Being of Knowledge." It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

name: Shining Lugia

attacks.name: Argent Wing
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has an Ability, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Aero Force
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It sleeps in a deep-sea trench. If it flaps its wings, it is said to cause a 40-day storm.

name: Mimikyu

abilities.name: Heal Jamming
abilities.text: Your opponent's Benched Pokémon can't be healed.

flavorText: It wears a rag fashioned into a Pikachu costume in an effort to look less scary. Unfortunately, the costume only makes it creepier.

name: Totodile

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack Totodile during your opponent's next turn. (Benching or evolving either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its well-developed jaws are powerful and capable of crushing anything. Even its trainer must be careful.

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Lock Up
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Holon Lake
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each player's Pokémon that has δ on its card can use attacks on this card instead of its own.

attacks.name: Delta Call
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon that has δ on its card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Vulpix and put it on Vulpix. (This counts as evolving Vulpix.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Chip Off
attacks.text: If your opponent has 6 or more cards in his or her hand, discard a number of cards without looking until your opponent has 5 cards left in his or her hand.

attacks.name: Poor Sleep
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage and Purugly G is now Asleep.

name: Unfezant

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Males swing their head plumage to threaten opponents. The females' flying abilities surpass those of the males.

name: Zoroark-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Trade
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Riotous Beating
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Trickster-GX
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Floating Electrons
abilities.text: As long as Voltorb has any Energy attached to it, Voltorb's Retreat Cost is 0.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Eevee does 10 damage to itself.

name: Professor Oak's Hint
rules: Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand. Your turn ends.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Quagsire

attacks.name: Fickle Impact
attacks.text: If you have exactly 2, 4, or 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It has an easygoing nature. It doesn't care if it bumps its head on boats and boulders while swimming.

name: Poliwag

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Poliwag but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavorText: Its newly grown legs prevent it from running. It appears to prefer swimming over trying to stand.

name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: They move in groups of a few individuals. A female is often the leader of the group, and the males will gather food.

name: Gallade

abilities.name: Premonition
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

attacks.name: Sensitive Blade
attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: A master of courtesy and swordsmanship, it fights using extending swords on its elbows.

name: Tyrogue
rules: If your Active Pokémon is a Baby Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.

attacks.name: Energy Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a Special Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Skyarrow Bridge
rules: The Retreat Cost of each Basic Pokémon is play is Colorless less.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: Remove all Special Conditions and 4 damage counters from Buneary. Buneary is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It slams foes by sharply uncoiling its rolled ears. It stings enough to make a grown-up cry in pain.

name: Marill

attacks.name: Rollout
attacks.text: If Marill is evolved from Azurill, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Splashing Turn
attacks.text: Switch Marill with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Using its tail as a float, it dives underwater. It likes eating plants that grow on river bottoms.

name: Durant

attacks.name: Devour
attacks.text: For each of your Durant in play, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves against Heatmor.

name: Virizion-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Verdant Wind
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Grass Energy attached to it can't be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting those Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Emerald Slash
attacks.text: You may search your deck for 2 Grass Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Espurr

attacks.name: Caturday
attacks.text: Draw a card. If you do, this Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Ear Kinesis
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon for each damage counter on that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The organ that emits its intense psychic power is sheltered by its ears to keep power from leaking out.

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Hattrem

attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brutal Swing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.

name: Metagross-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Geotech System
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Psychic or Metal Energy card from your discard pile to your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Giga Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Giga Hammer during your next turn.

attacks.name: Algorithm-GX
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 5 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Mr. Stone's Project
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. Or, search your discard pile for up to 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.

name: Liepard

attacks.name: Silent Claw
attacks.text: Your opponent reveal his or her hand. Discard a Supporter card you find there. Use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Their beautiful form comes from the muscles they have developed. They run silently in the night.

abilities.name: Cloud-Covered Moon
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Umbreon is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, return Umbreon and all cards attached to it to your hand. This power can't be used if Umbreon is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Evoblast
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Pokémon in play that evolves from Eevee.

name: Black Kyurem

attacks.name: Thunder Nail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Frozen Slice
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

name: Weedle

attacks.name: Rescue String
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 5 Pokémon, show them to your opponent, and shuffle them into your deck.

attacks.name: Needling Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It eats its weight in leaves every day. It fends off attackers with the needle on its head.

name: Empoleon

attacks.name: Total Command
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

name: Ferroseed

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.

flavorText: It absorbs the iron it finds in the rock while clinging to the ceiling. It shoots spikes when in danger.

name: Purrloin

attacks.name: Captivate
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Their cute act is a ruse. They trick people and steal their valuables just to see the looks on their faces.

name: Blissey

abilities.name: Softboiled
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, heal 30 damage from your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

name: Super Potion
rules: Discard 1 Energy card attached to your own Pokémon in order to remove up to 4 damage counters from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Darkness Howl
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) (excluding any Darkness Pokémon). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Power Claw
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Megaton Tail
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

name: Hoopa-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Scoundrel Ring
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon-EX (except for Hoopa-EX), reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Hyperspace Fury
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Aipom

attacks.name: Fiddle Around
attacks.text: Look at the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.

name: Swablu
flavorText: For some reason, it likes to land on people's heads softly and act like it's a hat.

name: Pidove
flavorText: Each follows its Trainer's orders as best it can, but they sometimes fail to understand complicated commands.

name: Audino-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Drain Slap
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Unown [T]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Tell]
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, look at your opponent's hand and show your hand to your opponent. This power can be used even if Unown [T] is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is believed that the variety of types of this Pokémon were created by evolutionary adaptation, as each possesses a different ability.

attacks.name: Super Speed
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Spearow during your opponent's next turn.

name: Lady Outing
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your deck for up to 3 different types of basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Pokémon Powers, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Professor Oak's Visit
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw 3 cards. Then, choose a card from your hand and put it on the bottom of your deck.

name: Nidoran ♂

attacks.name: Horn Hazard
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Stiffens its ears to sense danger. The larger, more powerful of its horns secretes venom.

name: Gible

attacks.name: Sand-Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It attacks using its huge mouth. While its attacks are powerful, it hurts itself out of clumsiness, too.

name: Trick Shovel
rules: Look at the top card of either player's deck. You may discard that card or return it to the top of the deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Bronzong

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Psychic Resonance
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Psychic Pokémon in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Ancient people believed that petitioning Bronzong for rain was the way to make crops grow.

name: Sinistea

attacks.name: Teatime
attacks.text: Each player draws 2 cards.

flavorText: This Pokémon is said to have been born when a lonely spirit possessed a cold, leftover cup of tea.

name: Sneasel

attacks.name: Hone Claws
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Slash attack's base damage is 80.

flavorText: It has a cunning yet savage disposition. It waits for parents to leave their nests, and then it sneaks in to steal their eggs.

name: Cynthia
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: PokéNav
rules: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck, and choose a Basic Pokémon, Evolution card, or Energy card. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Put the 2 other cards back on top of your deck in any order.

name: Double Colorless Energy
rules: Double Colorless Energy provides ColorlessColorless Energy.

name: Cobalion

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Iron Breaker
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It has a body and heart of steel. Its glare is sufficient to make even an unruly Pokémon obey it.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Direct-hit Bomb
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to Exeggcute. Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. For each heads, this attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.

name: Pokémon Reversal
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

name: Golett

attacks.name: Return
attacks.text: You may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.

flavorText: Its movements are powered by a mysterious energy. It has continued to move since ancient times, so its power may soon run out.

name: Cradily ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Primal Vibes
abilities.text: As long as Cradily ex is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't play a Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Eerie Light
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Acidic Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned and Burned.

name: Sharpedo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hunt
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jagged Fang
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. Then, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Balloon Barrage
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Their fur feels so good that if two of them snuggle together, they won't want to be separated.

name: Vibrava

attacks.name: Charge Energy
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The ultrasonic waves it generates by rubbing its two wings together cause severe headaches.

name: Porygon

attacks.name: Calculate
attacks.text: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

flavorText: The world's first artificially created Pokémon. It can travel through electronic space.

name: Indigo Plateau
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each Pokémon LEGEND in play (both yours and your opponent's) gets +30 HP.

name: Sableye

abilities.name: Excavate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top card of your deck. You may discard that card.

attacks.name: Disable
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: This Pokémon is feared. When its gemstone eyes begin to glow with a sinister shine, it's believed that Sableye will steal people's spirits away.

name: Pow! Hand Extension
rules: You may use this card only if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent.

Move 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon. Or, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

name: Spiritomb

attacks.name: Spirit Compressor
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 4 Pokémon and discard them. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It was bound to a fissure in an odd keystone as punishment for misdeeds 500 years ago

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Dream Eater
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon isn't Asleep, this attack does nothing.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

name: Registeel ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

attacks.name: Barrier Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Registeel Star by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Final Laser
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left and Registeel Star is the only Pokémon you have in play, put 6 damage counters instead.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Nuzzle Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Nuzzle attack to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It uses its tail to absorb electricity from power plants or from outlets in houses, and then it fires the electricity from its whiskers.

name: Ampharos ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Conductivity
abilities.text: As long as Ampharos ex is in play, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card to his or her Pokémon from hand, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. You can't put more than 1 damage counter even if there is more than 1 Ampharos ex in play.

attacks.name: Gigavolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Treecko

attacks.name: Call for Friends
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Poison Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

name: Darkness Energy
rules: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect unless the Attacking Pokémon is Darkness or has Dark in its name. Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a Basic card.)

name: Krookodile

attacks.name: False Accusation
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Obsidian Fang
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its unique faculty of sight can detect small prey more than 30 miles away, even in the midst of a sandstorm.

name: Golurk

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Fist of Antiquity
attacks.text: If you have any Supporter cards in your discard pile, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Some say that ancient people invented Golurk to serve as a laborer. It follows its master's orders faithfully.

name: Rocket's Admin.
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player counts his or her Prize cards left and draws up to that many cards. (You draw your cards first.)

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Grass Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Poison Spurt
attacks.text: Discard a Grass Energy card attached to Weedle. The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Altaria

attacks.name: Cleave
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard all Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

name: Mareep

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its fluffy coat swells to double when static electricity builds up. Touching it can be shocking.

name: Entei ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to Entei ex.

name: Double Colorless Energy
rules: Double Colorless Energy provides ColorlessColorless Energy.

name: Polteageist

attacks.name: Teatime
attacks.text: Each player draws 2 cards.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. This attack does 50 damage for each Trainer card you find there.

flavorText: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

attacks.name: Stretch Kick
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 30 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Heatran

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Boiling Impact
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

name: Kyogre & Groudon LEGEND
rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Kyogre & Groudon LEGEND.
When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mega Tidal Wave

name: Scraggy

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Each Pokémon with Dark or Rocket's in its name (both yours and your opponent's) gets +20 HP.

name: Rattata

name: Zorua

attacks.name: Hide in Shadows

name: Audino

attacks.name: Do the Wave

attacks.text: If Chinchou has any Water Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It discharges positive and negative electricity from its antenna tips to shock its foes.

name: Flabébé

name: Gabite

abilities.name: Dragon Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Dragon Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It loves sparkly things. It seeks treasures in caves and hoards the loot in its nest.

name: Celio's Network
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card (excluding Pokémon-ex), show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Rhyhorn

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack Rhyhorn during your opponent's next turn. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

flavorText: Its massive bones are 1,000 times harder than human bones. It can easily knock a trailer flying.

attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: Togekiss C does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.text: Search your deck for Silcoon and Beautifly, or Cascoon and Dustox cards. Show 1 card or both cards of a pair to your opponent and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Poison Barb

name: Phanpy

attacks.name: Strike Back
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is strong despite its compact size. It can easily pick up and carry an adult human on its back.

name: Marshadow

abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, discard this Pokémon and all cards attached to it.

attacks.name: Red Knuckles
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Ultra Beast, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It slips into the shadows of others and mimics their powers and movements. As it improves, it becomes stronger than those it's imitating.

name: Venonat

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It possesses a type of radar, which it can use to find the insects it eats for food, even in pitch darkness.

name: Voltorb

attacks.name: Outlet
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Bouncing Ball
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Voltorb does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on touch.

name: Vullaby
flavorText: They tend to guard their posteriors with suitable bones they have found. They pursue weak Pokémon.

name: Indeedee V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Watch Over
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The moonlight that it stores in the wings on its back apparently gives it the ability to float in midair.

name: Phantump
flavorText: By imitating the voice of a child, it causes people to get hopelessly lost deep in the forest. It's trying to make friends with them.

name: Polteageist

abilities.name: Tea Break
abilities.text: You must discard a Pokémon that has the Mad Party attack from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

name: Slowbro Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Slowbro-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Darkrai

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Dark Raid
attacks.text: If your opponent has already used their GX attack, this attack does 80 more damage.

name: Jirachi-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Stellar Guidance

attacks.name: Hypnostrike

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

flavorText: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

name: Meteor Falls
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Each player's Active Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) can use any attack from its Basic Pokémon or its Stage 1 Evolution card. (You still have to pay for that attack's Energy cost.)

name: Prism Energy
rules: This card provides Colorless Energy.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is a Basic Pokémon, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time.

name: Raikou ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dazzle Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Lightning Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Raikou ex does 20 damage to itself.

name: Slugma

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn't keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

name: Golem ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Magnitude
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Golem ex does 50 damage to itself.

name: Milcery

attacks.name: Aromatherapy
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

name: Azelf

attacks.name: Psypower
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Known as "The Being of Willpower." It sleeps at the bottom of a lake to keep the world in balance.

name: Geodude

attacks.name: Stone Throw
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Many live on mountain trails and remain half buried while keeping an eye on climbers.

name: Minccino

attacks.name: Tail Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Metallic Sound
attacks.text: Discard all Special Energy from each Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its feathers, which fall off as it grows, are thin and sharp. In times long past, warriors used them as swords.

name: Starly

attacks.name: Bug Search
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

flavorText: They flock in great numbers. Though small, they flap their wings with great power.

name: Remoraid

attacks.name: Trickle
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It forcefully squirts water. The water jet never misses prey even if the REMORAID is deep in the sea.

attacks.name: Double Razor Leaf
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Pompous Punch
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can't help grinning if anyone pats its head.

name: Eevee
flavorText: Because its genetic makeup is irregular, it quickly changes its form due to a variety of causes.

name: Scizor-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon's remaining HP is 100 or less, its attacks do 80 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 100 more damage. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Vullaby

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Tailwind
attacks.text: Attach an Energy from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Feather Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Mewtwo-GX

attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Hoothoot

attacks.name: Dual Draw
attacks.text: Each player draws 2 cards.
