attacks.name: Poison Sound Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Strength in Numbers
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Zubat, Golbat, and Crobat on your Bench.

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Super Speed
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Swablu during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Shoot Through
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its wings are like cotton tufts. If it perches on someone's head, it looks like a cotton hat.

name: Eevee

abilities.name: Energy Evolution
abilities.text: When you attach a basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon that is the same type as that Energy card and put it onto this Pokémon. (This counts as evolving this Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: A rare Pokémon that adapts to harsh environments by taking on different evolutionary forms.

name: Lysandre
rules: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Cinccino

attacks.name: Sweeping Cure
attacks.text: Heal 90 damage from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Cinccino's body is coated in a special oil that helps it deflect attacks, such as punches.

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

name: Lucian's Assignment
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Move as many Energy cards attached to 1 of your Pokémon as you like to another of your Pokémon.

name: Malamar

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Energy Slosh
attacks.text: You may move all Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants.

name: Bunnelby
flavorText: They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through.

name: Drakloak

attacks.name: Summon
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Dreepy and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It's capable of flying faster than 120 mph. It battles alongside Dreepy and dotes on them until they successfully evolve.

name: Herdier

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a black, cape-like fur that is very hard and decreases the amount of damage it receives.

name: Klink

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Rip Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Doom Crush
attacks.text: Discard a Darkness Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.

name: Unown

abilities.name: DAMAGE
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, and if there are 66 or more damage counters on your Benched Pokémon, you may use this Ability. If you do, you win this game.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

name: Slurpuff

abilities.name: Sweet Veil
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Fairy Energy attached to it can't be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting those Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

name: Boost Energy
rules: Boost Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon. Discard Boost Energy at the end of the turn it was attached. Boost Energy provides ColorlessColorlessColorless Energy. The Pokémon Boost Energy is attached to can't retreat. If the Pokémon Boost Energy is attached to isn't an Evolved Pokémon, discard Boost Energy.

name: Giratina-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

name: Great Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Zorua

attacks.name: Moonless Madness
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Dark Edge
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

name: Drifloon

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A Pokémon formed by the spirits of people and Pokémon. It loves damp, humid seasons.

name: Switch
rules: Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Morelull

attacks.name: Flickering Spores
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It scatters spores that flicker and glow. Anyone seeing these lights falls into a deep slumber.

name: Mt. Coronet
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may put 2 Metal Energy cards from their discard pile into their hand.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Arceus LV.X
rules: You may have as many of this card in your deck as you like.
Put this card onto your Active Arceus. Arceus LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Multitype
abilities.text: Arceus LV.X's type is the same as its previous Level.

attacks.name: Meteor Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack's base damage is 50 instead of 100.

name: Sky Field
rules: Each player can have 8 Pokémon on his or her Bench.
(When this card leaves play, each player discards Benched Pokémon until he or she has 5 Pokémon on the Bench. The owner of this card discards first.)
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Regigigas

abilities.name: Seal of Antiquity
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't attack unless Regirock, Regice, and Registeel are on your Bench.

attacks.name: Giant Stomp
attacks.text: Discard any Stadium card in play.

flavorText: It is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Best Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It's exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.

name: Lugia-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Overflow
abilities.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.name: Plasma Gale
attacks.text: Discard a Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you can't discard a Plasma Energy, this attack does nothing.

name: Surprise! Time Machine
rules: Choose 1 of your Evolved Pokémon, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from it, and shuffle it into your deck (this counts as devolving that Pokémon). If that Pokémon remains in play, search your deck for an Evolution card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon (this counts as evolving that Pokémon). Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Spritzee
flavorText: In the past, rather than using perfume, royal ladies carried a Spritzee that would waft a fragrance they liked.

name: Aggron ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Intimidating Armor
abilities.text: As long as Aggron ex is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Basic Pokémon can't attack or use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

attacks.name: Split Bomb
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack doe 30 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mega Burn
attacks.text: During your next turn, Aggron ex can't use Mega Burn.

name: Rattata
flavorText: Bites anything when it attacks. Small and very quick, it is a common sight in many places.

name: Pupitar

attacks.name: Rocket Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Even sealed in its shell, it can move freely. Hard and fast, it has outstanding destructive power.

name: Swampert ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hyper Pump
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each basic Energy attached to Swampert ex but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 80 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Crushing Wave
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. After doing damage, flip a coin. If heads, your opponent discards an Energy card, if any, attached to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Sandslash

attacks.name: Continuous Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its claws and horns often break off. The broken claws and horns can be used to carve plows for tilling farm fields.

name: EXP.ALL
rules: Attach EXP.ALL to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

During your opponent's turn, if your Active Pokémon would be Knocked Out by your opponent's attack, you may take 1 of the basic Energy cards attached to your Active Pokémon and attach it to the Pokémon with EXP.ALL attached to it. If you do, discard EXP.ALL.

name: Double Full Heal
rules: Remove all Special Conditions from each of your Active Pokémon.

name: Virizion

attacks.name: Double Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Leaf Wallop
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Leaf Wallop does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

name: Cinccino

attacks.name: Captivate
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fluffy Tail
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Their white fur feels amazing to touch. Their fur repels dust and prevents static electricity from building up.

name: Florges

abilities.name: Wondrous Gift
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, put an Item card from your discard pile on top of your deck.

attacks.name: Mist Guard
attacks.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Dragon Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: In times long past, governors of castles would invite Florges to create flower gardens to embellish the castle domains.

name: Skitty

attacks.name: Heal Bell
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Skitty does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It can't stop itself from chasing moving things, and it runs in a circle, chasing its own tail.

name: Ancient Technical Machine [Steel]
rules: Attach this card to 1 of your Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex and Pokémon that has an owner in its name) in play. That Pokémon may use this card's attack instead of its own. At the end of your turn, discard Ancient Technical Machine [Steel].

attacks.name: Steel Generator
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has a Poké-Power or Poké-Body, choose up to 2 basic Energy cards attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and attach them to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Root Fossil
rules: Play Root Fossil as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Root Fossil counts as a Colorless Pokémon (as well as a Trainer card). Root Fossil has no attacks of its own, can't retreat, and can't be affected by any Special Conditions. If Root Fossil is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. (Discard it anyway.) At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Root Fossil from play.

abilities.name: Spongy Stone
abilities.text: At any times between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Root Fossil.

name: Rhyhorn

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Rhyhorn does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its body is clad in a thick hide, and its tackles topple buildings. Unfortunately, it is not smart.

name: Whimsicott

attacks.name: The Wages of Fluff
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out during your next turn, take 2 more Prize cards.

flavorText: It rides on the wind and slips into people's homes. After it has turned a room into a cotton-filled mess, it giggles to itself and takes off.

name: Great Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Excadrill

ancientTrait.name: Ω Barrage
ancientTrait.text: This Pokémon may attack twice a turn. (If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent's Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Dredge
attacks.text: Search your deck for 2 Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: More than 300 feet below the surface, they build mazelike nests. Their activity can be destructive to subway tunnels.

name: Warp Point
rules: Your opponent switches 1 of his or her Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. You switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any.

name: Energy Retrieval
rules: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Lugia-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aero Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Deep Hurricane
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 70 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.

name: Mimikyu

abilities.name: Shadow Box
abilities.text: Pokémon-GX that have any damage counters on them (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.

attacks.name: Tail Trickery
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Although it's a quiet, lonely Pokémon, if you try to look at what's under its rag, it will become agitated and resist violently.

name: Staryu
flavorText: An enigmatic Pokémon that can effortlessly regenerate any appendage it loses in battle.

name: Granbull

attacks.name: Dark Clamp
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Fight Back
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: More timid than Snubbull, this Pokémon is doted on by young people amused at the contrast between its looks and its attitude.

name: Beldum

attacks.name: Single Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: With magnetic traction, it pulls its opponents in close. When they're in range, it slashes them with its rear claws.

name: Switch
rules: Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Kyurem-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frozen Wings
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hail Blizzard
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Hail Blizzard during your next turn.

name: Yveltal

attacks.name: Derail
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

name: Mysterious Fossil
rules: Play Mysterious Fossil as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Mysterious Fossil counts as a Colorless Pokémon (as well as a Trainer card). Mysterious Fossil has no attacks of its own, can't retreat, and can't be affected by any Special Conditions. If Mysterious Fossil is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. (Discard it anyway.) At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Mysterious Fossil from play.

name: Dome Fossil
rules: Play Dome Fossil as if it were a Colorless Basic Pokémon. (Dome Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Dome Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Dome Fossil can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Dome Fossil from play. (This doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Rock Reaction
abilities.text: When you attach a Fighting Energy card from your hand to Dome Fossil (excluding effects of attacks or Poké-Powers), search your deck for a card that evolves from Dome Fossil and put it onto Dome Fossil (this counts as evolving Dome Fossil). Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Kecleon

abilities.name: Color Change
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, this Pokémon is the same type as the your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Imittack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. If this Pokémon has the necessary Energy to use that attack, use it as this attack.

flavorText: It can freely change its body's color. The zigzag pattern on its belly doesn't change, however.

name: Latios ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Stream
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to Latios ex.

attacks.name: Luster Purge
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy attached to Latios ex.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Chansey does 40 damage to itself.

name: Eviolite
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is a Basic Pokémon, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Cacturne ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Cursed Glare
abilities.text: As long as Cacturne ex is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't attach any Special Energy cards (except for Darkness and Metal Energy cards) from his or her hand to his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Spike Rend
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Barrier Attack
attacks.text: All damage done by attacks to Sabrina's Porygon during your opponent's next turn is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Klang

attacks.name: Gear Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: A minigear and big gear comprises its body. If the minigear it launches at its foe doesn't return, it will die.

name: Munna

attacks.name: Future Sight
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of either player's deck and put them back in any order.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears before people and Pokémon who are having nightmares and eats those dreams.

name: Beldum

attacks.name: Pit Search
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It converses with others by using magnetic pulses. In a swarm, they move in perfect unison.

name: Fletchinder
flavorText: The hotter the flame sac on its belly, the faster it can fly, but it takes some time to get the fire going.

name: Larvitar

attacks.name: Mountain Bite
attacks.text: You may discard the top card of your deck. If you do, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage and Larvitar is now Asleep.

flavorText: A Pokémon that eats soil. Once it has eaten a large mountain, it goes to sleep so it can grow.

name: Double Colorless Energy
rules: Double Colorless Energy provides ColorlessColorless Energy.

name: Blaine's Quiz #1
rules: Put a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from your hand face down in front of you and tell your opponent its name. Your opponent guesses the length of that Pokémon. Flip the card over. If your opponent guessed right, he or she draws 2 cards. If your opponent guessed wrong, you draw 2 cards. Either way, return the card to your hand.

name: Dual Ball
rules: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Suicune & Entei LEGEND
rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Suicune & Entei LEGEND.
When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Torrent Blade
attacks.text: Return 2 Water Energy attached to Suicune & Entei LEGEND to your hand. Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 100 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Bursting Inferno
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Hoopa-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hyperspace Ring
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Wonder Trick
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Garchomp

attacks.name: Mach Cut
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragonblade
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

flavorText: It is said that when one runs at high speed, its wings create blades of wind that can fell nearby trees.

name: Hitmonlee

attacks.name: Coordinated Strike
attacks.text: If Hitmonchan is on your Bench, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The legs freely contract and stretch. The stretchy legs allow it to hit a distant foe with a rising kick.

name: Pokémon Contest Hall
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Once during each player's turn, if that player's Bench isn't full, the player may flip a coin. If heads, that player searches his or her deck for a Basic Pokémon and puts it onto his or her Bench. If the player does, he or she may search his or her deck for a Pokémon Tool card and attach it to that Pokémon. If that player searched his or her deck, the player shuffles his or her deck afterward.

attacks.name: Fling
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

name: Slakoth

attacks.name: Big Yawn
attacks.text: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.

flavorText: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

name: Psyduck

attacks.name: Delusion
attacks.text: If Psyduck is Confused, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Migraine
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, Psyduck is now Confused.

flavorText: If its usual headache worsens, its starts exhibiting odd powers. It can't remember doing so, however.

abilities.name: Forest Breath
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Celebi is your Active Pokémon, you may attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Celebi is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Time Circle
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to Celebi by attacks from your opponent's Stage 1 or Stage 2 Pokémon.

name: Pokémon Center Lady
rules: Heal 60 damage and remove all Special Conditions from 1 of your Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Mary
rules: Draw 2 cards. Then, shuffle 2 cards from your hand into your deck.

name: Diggersby

attacks.name: Smithereen Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: With their powerful ears, they can heft boulders of a ton or more with ease. They can be a big help at construction sites.

name: Ralts

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

name: Tangela

attacks.name: Vine Invite
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Grass Knot
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Colorless Energy in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the the Retreat Cost).

flavorText: It is shrouded by blue vines. No one has seen the face hidden behind this growth of vines.

name: Dragapult

abilities.name: Infiltrator
abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.name: Phantom Force
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: When it isn't battling, it keeps Dreepy in the holes on its horns. Once a fight starts, it launches the Dreepy like supersonic missiles.

name: Holon Energy FF
rules: Holon Energy FF provides Colorless Energy.

If the Pokémon that Holon Energy FF is attached to also has a basic Fire Energy card attached to it, that Pokémon has no Weakness. If the Pokémon that Holon Energy FF is attached to also has a basic Fighting Energy card attached to it, damage done by that Pokémon's attack isn't affected by Resistance. Ignore these effects if Holon Energy FF is attached to Pokémon-ex.

name: Kirlia
flavorText: The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.

name: Starly
flavorText: Because they are weak individually, they form groups. However, they bicker if the group grows too big.

name: Cutiefly

attacks.name: Fly Around
attacks.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

flavorText: It feeds on the nectar and pollen of flowers. Because it's able to sense auras, it can identify which flowers are about to bloom.

name: Luxury Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon (excluding Pokémon LV.X), show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. If any Luxury Ball is in your discard pile, you can't play this card.

name: Dhelmise

attacks.name: Seaweed Grab
attacks.text: Put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Buster Swing
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It wraps its prey in green seaweed and sucks away their vitality. It only likes to go after big prey like Wailord.

name: Darkness Energy
rules: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect unless the Attacking Pokémon is Darkness or has Dark in its name. Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Float
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Chinchou.

name: Stoutland

attacks.name: Special Fang
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has a Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Ferocious Bellow

rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

name: Rufflet

Shuffle your deck afterward.

rules: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Vigoroth

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Deep Growl

Root Fossil has no attacks of its own, can't retreat, and can't be affected by any Special Conditions. If Root Fossil is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. (Discard it anyway.) At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Root Fossil from play.

abilities.name: Spongy Stone
abilities.text: At any time between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Root Fossil.

rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Any damage done by attacks from Lightning Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) to the Defending Pokémon isn't affected by Resistance. Each Lightning Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) has no Weakness.

name: Buizel

rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your discard pile for 3 basic Energy cards and any combination of 3 Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards, show them to your opponent, and put them on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

rules: Attach Lum Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.

rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If the Pokémon that Holon Energy GL is attached to also has a basic Grass Energy card attached to it, that Pokémon can't be affected by any Special Conditions. If the Pokémon that Holon Energy GL is attached to also has a basic Lightning Energy card attached to it, damage done to that Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon-ex is reduced by 10. Ignore these effects if Holon Energy GL is attached to Pokémon-ex.

name: Bulbasaur
flavorText: For some time after its birth, it grows by gaining nourishment from the seed on its back.

abilities.name: Catastrophe
abilities.text: As long as Gengar is your Active Pokémon, if any of your opponent's Pokémon would be Knocked Out, put that Pokémon in the Lost Zone instead of discarding. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Hurl into Darkness
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand and choose a number of Pokémon you find there up to the number of Psychic Energy attached to Gengar. Put the Pokémon you chose in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Zoroark

abilities.name: Stand In
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

name: Terrakion

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Earthen Power
attacks.text: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It has phenomenal power. It will mercilessly crush anyone or anything that bullies small Pokémon.

name: Desert Ruins
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
At any time between turns, each player puts 1 damage counter on his or her Pokémon-ex with maximum HP of at least 100.

name: Slaking

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Knuckle Sandwich
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The world's laziest Pokémon. It moves to another spot when there's no food left within its reach.

name: Rare Candy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 1 or Stage 2 card that evolves from that Pokémon in your hand, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.)

name: Minccino

attacks.name: Tickle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Minccino greet each other by grooming one another thoroughly with their tails.

name: Staravia

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

name: Dugtrio

attacks.name: Home Ground
attacks.text: If you have any Stadium card in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: While the three of them normally get along splendidly, on rare occasions a huge fight will break out over which head gets to eat first.

name: Muk ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Toxic Gas
abilities.text: As long as Muk ex is your Active Pokémon, ignore all Poké-Powers and Poké-Bodies other than Toxic Gas.

attacks.name: Poison Breath
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Slimy Water
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Colorless Energy in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost).

name: Primal Kyogre-EX
rules: Primal Reversion rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes Primal Kyogre-EX, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ancientTrait.name: θ Max
ancientTrait.text: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes this Pokémon, heal all damage from it.

attacks.name: Tidal Storm
attacks.text: Move 2 Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon-EX. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Incineroar-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Scar Charge
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put 3 damage counters on this Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for up to 3 Darkness Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Crushing Punch
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Darkest Tornado-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Super Energy Retrieval
rules: Trade 2 of the other cards in your hand for 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile. If you have fewer than 4 basic Energy cards there, take all of them.

name: Venture Bomb
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If tails, put 1 damage counter on 1 of your Pokémon.

name: Metal Energy
rules: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn't Metal. Metal Energy provides Metal Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Dragalge

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck.

flavorText: Tales are told of ships that wander into seas where Dragalge live, never to return.

name: Switch
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Tapu Fini

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Shining Current
attacks.text: If any of your Water Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The dense fog it creates brings the downfall and destruction of its confused enemies. Ocean currents are the source of its energy.

name: Rhyhorn
flavorText: It can remember only one thing at a time. Once it starts rushing, it forgets why it started.

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Alph Lithograph
rules: Shuffle your deck!

name: Helix Fossil
rules: Play Helix Fossil as if it were a Colorless Basic Pokémon. (Helix Fossil counts as a Trainer card as well, but if Helix Fossil is Knocked Out, this counts as a Knocked Out Pokémon.) Helix Fossil can't be affected by any Special Conditions and can't retreat. At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Helix Fossil from play. (This doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Aqua Reaction
abilities.text: When you attach a Water Energy card from your hand to Helix Fossil (excluding effects of attacks or Poké-Powers), search your deck for a card that evolves from Helix Fossil and put it onto Helix Fossil (this counts as evolving Helix Fossil). Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Haxorus

attacks.name: Stunning Uppercut
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Their sturdy tusks will stay sharp even if used to cut steel beams. These Pokémon are covered in hard armor.

name: Blaine
rules: During this turn, instead of attaching your free Energy card, you may instead attach 2 Fire Energy cards to 1 of your Pokémon with Blaine in its name. (Playing additional copies of this card this turn has no effect.)

name: Radio Tower
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Once during each player's turn (before attacking), that player may look at the top 2 cards of his or her deck and put them back in the same order.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Electrichain
attacks.text: If you have any Lightning Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It uses its tail to absorb electricity from power plants or from outlets in houses, and then it fires the electricity from its whiskers.

name: Flygon

abilities.name: Sand Slammer
abilities.text: At any time between turns, if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flying Beatdown
attacks.text: You may discard a Grass Energy and a Fighting Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Known as "The Desert Spirit" this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

attacks.name: Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: M Swampert-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strongarm Impact
attacks.text: You may do 30 more damage. If you do, discard the top 3 cards of each player's deck.

name: Slakoth

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Slakoth.

attacks.name: Slack Off
attacks.text: Remove all damage counters from Slakoth. Slakoth can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: It spends nearly all its time in a day sprawled out. Just seeing it makes one drowsy.

name: Ribombee

abilities.name: Curative Pollen
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It rolls up pollen into puffs. It makes many different varieties, some used as food and others used in battle.

name: Kirlia
flavorText: The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.

name: Furfrou

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

name: Zweilous

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

name: Deino

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: They cannot see, so they tackle and bite to learn about their surroundings. Their bodies are covered in wounds.

name: Tapu Bulu-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Nature's Judgment
attacks.text: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Tapu Wilderness-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Shadow Hand
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 cards from your hand. If you do, draw that many cards.

name: Tapu Lele

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Magical Swap
attacks.text: Move any number of damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon to their other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This guardian deity of Akala is guilelessly cruel. The fragrant aroma of flowers is the source of its energy.

name: Marley's Request
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your discard pile for 2 different Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium cards, show them to your opponent, and your opponent chooses 1 of them. Put that card into your hand, and discard the other card. (If all Trainer, Supporter, and Stadium cards in your discard pile have the same name, choose 1 of them. Show that card to your opponent and put it into your hand.)

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: All damage done by attacks to Metapod is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: M Charizard-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wild Blaze
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck.

name: Elgyem

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It controls tremendous psychic power. Most reports of alien sightings are actually just people mistaking Elgyem for an alien.

name: Metagross

abilities.name: Extend
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your turn does not end when you play Steven's Resolve.

attacks.name: Meteor Mash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Meteor Mash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A linkage of two Metang, this Pokémon can perform any calculation in a flash by utilizing parallel processing in its four brains.

name: Mewtwo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: X Ball
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psydrive
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Holon Lass
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Discard a card from your hand. If you can't discard a card from your hand, you can't play this card.

Count the total number of Prize cards left (both yours and your opponent's). Look at that many cards from the top of your deck, choose as many Energy cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Armor Fossil Shieldon
rules: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Shieldon you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Trapinch

attacks.name: Land's Pulse
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Its nest is a sloped, bowl-like pit in the desert. Once something has fallen in, there is no escape.

name: Seedot

attacks.name: Bide
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, if Seedot would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, Seedot is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

flavorText: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

name: Xerneas-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Break Through
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: X Blast
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use X Blast during your next turn.

name: Darkness Energy
rules: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect unless the Attacking Pokémon is Darkness or has Dark in its name. Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Torchic

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees F.

name: Delcatty ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Constrain
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Each player discards cards until that player has 6 cards in his or her hand. (You discard first.) This power can't be used if Delcatty ex is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Upstream
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for all Energy cards. This attack does 10 damage times the number of Energy cards you find there. Show them to your opponent, and put them on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Durant

attacks.name: Hard Crunch
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Individuals each play different roles in driving Heatmor, their natural predator, away from their colony.

abilities.name: Conductivity
abilities.text: Whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from his or her hand to 1 of his or her Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lightning Crush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage. If tails, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Plume Fossil
rules: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Archen you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Mt. Moon
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Any Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) with maximum HP less than 70 can't use any Poké-Powers.

name: Cosmog

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Even though its helpless, gaseous body can be blown away by the slightest breeze, it doesn't seem to care.

name: Genesect

attacks.name: Signal Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Overdrive Smash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overdrive Smash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Magikarp.

attacks.name: Dragon Rage
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It is said to be the world's weakest Pokémon. No one knows why it has managed to survive.

name: Gengar LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Gengar. Gengar LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Level-Down
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon LV.X. Remove the Level-Up card from that Pokémon and have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck. The power can't be used if Gengar is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Compound Pain
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon that already has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Rufflet

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.

name: Team Galactic's Invention G-107 Technical Machine G
rules: Attach this card to 1 of your Pokémon SP in play. That Pokémon may use this card's attack instead of its own. When the Pokémon this card is attached to is no longer Pokémon SP, discard this card.

attacks.name: Damage Porter
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on the Pokémon this card is attached to to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Scramble Energy
rules: Scramble Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex). Scramble Energy provides Colorless Energy. While in play, if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, Scramble Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 3 in any combination at a time. If the Pokémon Scramble Energy is attached to isn't an Evolved Pokémon (or evolves into Pokémon-ex), discard Scramble Energy.

name: Potion
rules: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if that Pokémon has only 1).

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Light Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It sails on the winds with its limbs extended to strike from the sky. It aims for the prey's face.

name: Sandshrew

attacks.name: Sand-attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Burrow deep underground in arid locations far from water. It only emerges to hunt for food.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Erika
rules: You may draw up to 3 cards, then your opponent may draw up to 3 cards.

name: Dragapult VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Phantom
attacks.text: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Deoxys ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Form Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Deoxys ex and switch it with Deoxys ex. (Any cards attached to Deoxys ex, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys ex on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Fastwave
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Unown

abilities.name: MISSING
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, and if your opponent has 12 or more Supporter cards in the Lost Zone, you may use this Ability. If you do, you win this game.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Calm Strike
attacks.text: If you have used your GX attack, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its metal body is sturdy, but it does rust rather easily. So on rainy days, this Pokémon prefers to stay put in its nest.

name: Dratini

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Dratini, a Dragonair, and a Dragonite, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: After a 10-hour struggle, a fisherman was able to pull one up and confirm its existence.

name: Rocky Helmet
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Team Galactic's Invention G-109 SP Radar
rules: Choose a card from your hand and put it on top of your deck. Search your deck for a Pokémon SP, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. (If this is the only card in your hand, you can't play this card.)

abilities.name: Blissful Nurse
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Blissey from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may remove all damage counters from all of your Pokémon. If you do, discard all Energy attached to those Pokémon that had any damage counters on them.

name: Lt. Surge
rules: You can play this card only if you have at least 1 Basic Pokémon card in your hand. Put a Basic Pokémon card from your hand into play as your Active Pokémon. Put your old Active Pokémon onto your Bench. (You can't play this card if your Bench is full.)

name: Poké Blower +
rules: You may play 2 Poké Blower + at the same time. If you play 1 Poké Blower +, flip a coin. If heads, put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If you play 2 Poké Blower +, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Exploud ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Extra Noise
abilities.text: As long as Exploud ex is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon-ex between turns.

attacks.name: Derail
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy card, if any, attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hyper Tail
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: M Kangaskhan-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wham Bam Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

name: Chespin
flavorText: Such a thick shell of wood covers its head and back that even a direct hit from a truck wouldn't faze it.

name: Metal Energy
rules: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn't Metal. Metal Energy provides Metal Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Wally's Training
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Search your deck for a card that evolves from your Active Pokémon (choose 1 if there are 2) and put it on your Active Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Salamence LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Salamence. Salamence LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Double Fall
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Salamence LV.X from your hand onto your Active Salamence, you may use this power. For each of your opponent's Pokémon that is Knocked Out by damage from Salamence's attacks this turn, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.name: Steam Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card attached to Salamence.

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: Switch Buneary with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mini Drain
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Buneary.

flavorText: Its ears are always rolled up. They can be forcefully extended to shatter even a large boulder.

name: Island Cave
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Whenever any player attaches an Energy card from his or her hand to Water Pokémon, Fighting Pokémon, or Metal Pokémon, remove any Special Conditions from that Pokémon.

name: Poké Drawer +
rules: You may play 2 Poké Drawer + at the same time. If you play 1 Poké Drawer +, draw a card. If you play 2 Poké Drawer +, search your deck for up to 2 cards, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Leafeon

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It basically does not fight. With cells similar to those of plants, it can perform photosynthesis.

name: Trainers' Mail
rules: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Trainer card you find there (except Trainers' Mail) and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Angry Horn
attacks.text: If there are 4 or more damage counters on Heracross, this attack's base damage is 50 instead of 30.

name: Underground Expedition
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Look at the 4 cards from the bottom of your deck. Choose any 2 cards there and put them into your hand. Put the remaining cards back on the bottom of your deck in any order.

name: Zygarde

attacks.name: Core Avenger
attacks.text: If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Born when all of Zygarde's cells have been gathered together, it uses force to neutralize those who harm the ecosystem.

name: Beheeyem

attacks.name: Mysterious Noise
attacks.text: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. Your opponent can't play any Item cards from their hand during their next turn.

flavorText: With its psychic powers, it rewrites its opponents' memories. You, too, may have already had your memories rewritten.

name: Time Capsule
rules: Your opponent may choose 5 Basic Pokémon, Evolution, and/or basic Energy cards in his or her discard pile. (If your opponent doesn't have that many, he or she chooses all or none of them.) If your opponent chooses any cards, he or she shuffles them into his or her deck. Either way, you may do the same, and you can't play any more Trainer cards this turn.

name: Dusclops ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Hole
abilities.text: As long as Dusclops ex is on your Bench, don't apply Darkness Weakness for all of your Pokémon is play.

attacks.name: Shadow Beam
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon for each Energy attached to Dusclops ex.

name: Captivating Poké Puff
rules: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Put any number of Basic Pokémon you find there onto your opponent's Bench.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Patrat

attacks.name: Safety Check
attacks.text: Look at 1 of your face-down Prize cards.

flavorText: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

name: Rattata

attacks.name: Dangerous Suspicion
attacks.text: Draw a card. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its fangs are long and very sharp. They grow continuously, so it gnaws on hard things to whittle them down.

name: Groudon ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Hard Rock
abilities.text: As long as Groudon ex has 1 Energy or less attached to it, damage done to any of your Groudon ex in play by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't use more than 1 Hard Rock Poké-Body each turn.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to Groudon ex.

name: Misty
rules: Discard 2 of the other cards in your hand in order to play this card. If this turn's attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and if the attacking Pokémon has Misty in its name, the attack does 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Tornadus-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blow Through
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Multi Energy
rules: Attach Multi Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Multi Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.) Multi Energy provides Colorless Energy when attached to a Pokémon that already has Special Energy cards attached to it.

name: Tangrowth LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Tangrowth. Tangrowth LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Healing Growth
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, remove 4 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Tangrowth is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Big Growth
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for as many Grass Energy cards as you like and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Call Energy
rules: Call Energy provides Colorless Energy. Once during your turn, if the Pokémon Call Energy is attached to is your Active Pokémon, you may search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. If you do, shuffle your deck and your turn ends.

name: Dragonair

attacks.name: Dragon's Wish
attacks.text: During your next turn, you may attach any number of Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon.

flavorText: From time immemorial, it has been venerated by agricultural peoples as an entity able to control the weather.

name: Aegislash

abilities.name: Miracle Guard
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon has no Weakness.

attacks.name: Protect Charge
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Apparently, it can detect the innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to become king.

name: Geodude

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Geodude that have lived a long life have had all their edges smoothed out until they're totally round. They also have a calm, quiet disposition.

name: Crobat ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Distortion
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Crobat ex is your Active Pokémon, you may put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This power can't be used if Crobat ex is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Cross Attack
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. If you get 2 or more heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Pester
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 damage plus 40 more damage.

name: Squirtle
flavorText: It shelters itself in its shell, then strikes back with spouts of water at every opportunity.

name: Regigigas-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Giga Power
attacks.text: You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Raging Hammer
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Zweilous

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

name: Registeel

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Silver Fist
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has an Ability, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Its body is said to be harder than any kind of metal. A study has revealed that its body is hollow.

name: Deoxys

attacks.name: Cell Storm
attacks.text: Discard 2 Psychic Energy attached to Deoxys and remove 6 damage counters from Deoxys.

flavorText: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

name: Brock's Protection
rules: Attach Brock's Protection to 1 of your Pokémon with Brock in its name. Energy cards attached to that Pokémon can't be removed by your opponent's attacks or Trainer cards. (This doesn't stop the rest of the attack or Trainer card from working normally.)

name: Claw Fossil
rules: Play Claw Fossil as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Claw Fossil counts as a Colorless Pokémon (instead of a Trainer card). Claw Fossil has no attacks of its own, can't retreat, and can't be affected by any Special Conditions. If Claw Fossil is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. (Discard it anyway.) At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Claw Fossil from play.

abilities.name: Jagged Stone
abilities.text: If Claw Fossil is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Claw Fossil is Knocked Out), put 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.

name: Doduo

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A two-headed Pokémon that was discovered as a sudden mutation. It runs at a pace of over 60 miles per hour.

attacks.name: Teary Eyes
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Cubone by attacks is reduced by 20.

name: Marshadow

attacks.name: Shadowy Echoes
attacks.text: Put a Basic Pokémon from each player's discard pile onto its owner's Bench.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Able to conceal itself in shadows, it never appears before humans, so its very existence was the stuff of myth.

name: Vibrava

attacks.name: Sand Pulse
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The ultrasonic waves it generates by rubbing its two wings together cause severe headaches.

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card, show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Black Kyurem

attacks.name: Dual Claw
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Flash Freeze
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Electric Ring
attacks.text: If Hoopa is on your Bench, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It has small electric sacs on both its cheeks. If threatened, it looses electric charges from the sacs.

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It sails on the winds with its limbs extended to strike from the sky. It aims for the prey's face.

name: Dark Metal Energy
rules: Attach Dark Metal Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Dark Metal Energy provides Darkness Energy and Metal Energy, but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play and has no effect other than providing Energy.)

name: Rayquaza ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spiral Growth
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to Rayquaza ex.

attacks.name: Dragon Burst
attacks.text: Discard either all Fire Energy or all Lightning Energy attached to Rayquaza ex. This attack does 40 damage times the amount of Fire or Lightning Energy discarded.

name: Staraptor

attacks.name: Strong Breeze
attacks.text: Your opponent shuffles the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck.

flavorText: It never stops attacking even if it is injured. It fusses over the shape of its comb.

name: AZ
rules: Put 1 Pokémon into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Cosmog

abilities.name: Unaware
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Even though its helpless, gaseous body can be blown away by the slightest breeze, it doesn't seem to care.

name: Braviary

attacks.name: Brave Bird
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: The more scars they have, the more respect these brave soldiers of the sky get from their peers.

name: Wobbuffet

abilities.name: Shady Tail
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, ◇ (Prism Star) Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) can't attack and have no Abilities.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it live quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

name: Shinx

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

name: Tapu Fini-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aqua Ring
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Shot
attacks.text: Discard 2 Water Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Tapu Storm-GX
attacks.text: Shuffle your opponent's Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Chikorita

attacks.name: Run Around
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Running around, climbing trees, playing hide-and-seek! Another wonderful day begins!

name: Multi Energy
rules: Attach Multi Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Multi Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.) Multi Energy provides Colorless Energy when attached to a Pokémon that already has Special Energy cards attached to it.

name: Rattata

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Cautious in the extreme, its hardy vitality lets it live in any kind of environment.

name: Voltorb
flavorText: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

name: Seel
flavorText: The protruding horn on its head is very hard. This horn is used for bashing through thick ice.

name: Audino Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Audino-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Galarian Farfetch'd

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The Farfetch'd of the Galar region are brave warriors, and they wield thick, tough leeks in battle.

name: Murkrow

attacks.name: Swarm
attacks.text: Search your deck for Murkrow and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Hide Crowd
attacks.text: Switch Murkrow with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is believed that seeing this Pokémon at night will bring about ominous occurrences.

name: Primal Groudon-EX
rules: Primal Reversion rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes Primal Groudon-EX, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ancientTrait.name: θ Max
ancientTrait.text: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes this Pokémon, heal all damage from it.

attacks.name: Gaia Volcano
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 100 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.

name: Gardevoir-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Secret Spring
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Fairy Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Infinite Force
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Twilight-GX
attacks.text: Shuffle 10 cards from your discard pile into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches the Defending Pokémon with it. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

name: Holon Mentor
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Discard a card from your hand. If you can't discard a card from your hand, you can't play this card.
Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon that each has 100 HP or less, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
