attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Withdraw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Squirtle during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: After birth, its back swells and hardens into a shell. It powerfully sprays foam from its mouth.

name: Enhanced Hammer
rules: Discard a Special Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Igglybuff
rules: If your Active Pokémon is a Baby Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.

attacks.name: Energy Heal
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your Pokémon with an Energy card attached to it. Remove 1 damage counter from that Pokémon.

name: Prof. Oak's Research
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 5 cards.

name: Starly

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: They flock in great numbers. Though small, they flap their wings with great power.

name: PokéNav
rules: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck, and choose a Basic Pokémon, Evolution card, or Energy card. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Put the 2 other cards back on top of your deck in any order.

abilities.name: Afterburner
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. This power can't be used if Typhlosion is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Flare Destroy
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card attached to Typhlosion and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Glaceon

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Reflect Energy
attacks.text: Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: By controlling its body heat, it can freeze the atmosphere around it to make a diamond-dust flurry.

name: Chimchar

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It is very agile. Before going to sleep, it extinguishes the flame on its tail to prevent fires.

name: Holon Scientist
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Discard a card from your hand. If you can't discard a card from your hand, you can't play this card.
If you have less cards in your hand than your opponent, draw cards until you have the same number of cards as your opponent.

name: Giovanni
rules: Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play with Giovanni in its name. For the rest of your turn, you may evolve that Pokémon even if you just played or evolved it this turn or if this is your first turn. This effect also applies to the Pokémon it evolves into.

name: Terrakion

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Its charge is strong enough to break through a giant castle wall in one blow. This Pokémon is spoken of in legends.

name: Torkoal

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Flame Ball
attacks.text: Move an Energy card attached to Torkoal to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

name: Zoroark

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Foul Play
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

flavorText: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

name: Upper Energy
rules: Upper Energy provides Colorless Energy. If you have more Prize cards left than your opponent and this card is attached to a Pokémon (excluding Pokémon LV.X), Upper Energy provides ColorlessColorless.

name: Steven's Advice
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw a number of cards up to the number of your opponent's Pokémon in play.
If you have more than 7 cards (including this one) in your hand, you can't play this card.

name: Medicham

attacks.name: Yoga Kick
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe's next move.

name: Gabite

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Shiny objects are its passion. It can be found in its cave, scarcely moving, its gaze fixed on the jewels it's amassed or Carbink it has caught.

name: Frozen City
rules: Whenever any player attaches an Energy from his or her hand to 1 of his or her Pokémon (excluding Team Plasma Pokémon) put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: White Kyurem-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Stream
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ice Burn
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Charge Up
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to Eevee. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Battle Reporter
rules: Draw cards until you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Marshadow

attacks.name: Shadow Imitation
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's non-GX attacks and use it as this attack.

flavorText: It slips into the shadows of others and mimics their powers and movements. As it improves, it becomes stronger than those it's imitating.

name: Deoxys ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Form Change
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Deoxys ex and switch it with Deoxys ex. (Any cards attached to Deoxys ex, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Deoxys ex on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Form Change Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Psychic Shield
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Deoxys ex by your opponent's Pokémon-ex during your opponent's next turn.

name: Sceptile ex δ
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Extra Liquid
abilities.text: Each player's Pokémon-ex can't use any Poké-Powers and pays Colorless more Energy to use its attacks. Each Pokémon can't be affected by more than 1 Extra Liquid Poké-Body.

attacks.name: Power Revenge
attacks.text: Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Gumshoos

attacks.name: Identify
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand. If you find a Pokémon there, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It adores having Rattata and Raticate for dinner, but as it's diurnal, it never encounters them. This Pokémon boasts incredible patience.

name: Trainers' Mail
rules: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Trainer card you find there (except Trainers' Mail) and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Scramble Energy
rules: Scramble Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex). Scramble Energy provides Colorless Energy. While in play, if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, Scramble Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 3 in any combination at a time. If the Pokémon Scramble Energy is attached to isn't an Evolved Pokémon (or evolves into Pokémon-ex), discard Scramble Energy.

name: Greedy Dice
rules: You can play this card only if you took it as a face-down Prize card, before you put it into your hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, take 1 more Prize card.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Energy Stadium
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Once during each player's turn (before attacking), that player may flip a coin. If heads, that player puts a basic Energy card from his or her discard pile into his or her hand.

name: Weedle

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It eats its weight in leaves every day. It fends off attackers with the needle on its head.

name: Hippopotas

attacks.name: Double Stomp
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It moves through the sands with its mouth open, swallowing sand along with its prey. It gets rid of the sand by spouting it from its nose.

name: Potion
rules: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if that Pokémon has only 1).

name: Cubone

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Beat attack's base damage is 80.

flavorText: At night, it weeps loudly for its dead mother, but those cries only attract its natural enemy—Mandibuzz.

name: Tauros-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mad Bull-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Devolution Spray
rules: Devolve 1 of your evolved Pokémon and put the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your hand. (That Pokémon can't evolve this turn.)
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Lugia-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Overflow
abilities.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.name: Plasma Gale
attacks.text: Discard a Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you can't discard a Plasma Energy, this attack does nothing.

abilities.name: Insight
abilities.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, the attack cost of each of Yanmega's attacks is 0.

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Sonicboom
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Bronzong

attacks.name: Pain Amplifier
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Ancient people believed that petitioning Bronzong for rain was the way to make crops grow.

name: Baltoy
flavorText: It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

name: Cerulean City Gym
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Each player pays Colorless less to retreat his or her Pokémon if it has Misty in its name.

name: Aegislash

abilities.name: Durable Blade
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, put it into your hand instead of the discard pile. (Discard all cards attached to it.)

attacks.name: Trash Slash
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage for each Item card in your discard pile. You can't do more than 130 damage in this way.

flavorText: Generations of kings were attended by these Pokémon, which used their spectral power to manipulate and control people and Pokémon.

name: Morelull
flavorText: It scatters spores that flicker and glow. Anyone seeing these lights falls into a deep slumber.

name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Rigidify
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey.

name: Crushing Hammer
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Hitmonlee

attacks.name: Gut Kick
attacks.text: If Tyrogue is anywhere under Hitmonlee, you may do 30 damage to any 1 Benched Pokémon instead. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its legs can stretch double. First-time foes are startled by its extensible reach.

name: Trevenant

abilities.name: Nervous Seed
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, the attacks of your opponent's Basic Pokémon cost Colorless more.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Using its roots as a nervous system, it controls the trees in the forest. It's kind to the Pokémon that reside in its body.

name: Froslass

abilities.name: Drag Along
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, flip a coin. If heads, the Attacking Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Snowy Drop
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Spring is already here, so goodbye…

name: Professor Birch
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Draw cards from your deck until you have 6 cards in your hand.

name: Giovanni's Last Resort
rules: Remove all damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon with Giovanni in its name. Then discard your hand.

name: Glaceon LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Glaceon. Glaceon LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Chilly Breath
abilities.text: As long as Glaceon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Pokémon can't use any Poké-Powers.

attacks.name: Avalanche
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Pikachu

abilities.name: Electro Recycle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Pichu is anywhere under Pikachu, you may search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. This power can't be used if Pikachu is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: BikaBika
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in forests with others. It stores electricity in the pouches on its cheeks.

name: Miracle Berry
rules: Attach Miracle Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

At any time between turns, if the Pokémon Miracle Berry is attached to is Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned, you may remove all those effects from that Pokémon and discard Miracle Berry. At the start of each turn, if the Pokémon Miracle Berry is attached to is Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned, remove all of those effects from that Pokémon and discard Miracle Berry.

name: Holon Transceiver
rules: Search your deck for a Supporter card that has Holon in its name, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. Or, search your discard pile for a Supporter card that has Holon in its name, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

name: Mightyena ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Driving Howl
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon and switch it with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Benched Pokémon to switch. This power can't be used if Mightyena ex is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hyper Claws
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon, this attack does 50 damage plus 40 more damage.

name: Mr. Mime

abilities.name: Bench Barrier
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks.

attacks.name: Juggling
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is adept at conning people. It is said to be able to create walls out of thin air by miming.

name: Giratina

abilities.name: Distortion Door
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put it onto your Bench. If you do, put 1 damage counter on 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Impact
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.

name: Imposter Professor Oak
rules: Your opponent shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck, then draws 7 cards.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Glare and Peck
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: For some reason, it likes to land on people's heads softly and act like it's a hat.

name: Hakamo-o
flavorText: It leaps at its prey with a courageous shout. Its scaly punches tear its opponents to shreds.

name: Silvally-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Gyro Unit
abilities.text: Your Basic Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Turbo Drive
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rebel-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Blissey ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Blissful Support
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Blissey ex from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may discard all Energy cards attached to any number of your Pokémon and remove all damage counters from those Pokémon.

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.text: Attach up to 3 Energy cards from your discard pile to Blissey ex.

name: Cobalion

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Iron Breaker
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It has a body and heart of steel. Its glare is sufficient to make even an unruly Pokémon obey it.

name: Altaria ex δ
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Extra Boost
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Stage 2 Pokémon-ex. This power can't be used if Altaria ex is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Healing Light
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon.

name: Mareanie
flavorText: They eat Corsola branches, so Mareanie are hated by craftsmen who work with Corsola branches that have naturally fallen off.

name: Arceus
rules: You may have as many of this card in your deck as you like.

attacks.name: Leaf Refresh
attacks.text: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to have emerged from an egg in a place where there was nothing, then shaped the world.

name: Chingling

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Chimecho from your hand onto Chingling (this counts as evolving Chingling) and remove all damage counters from Chingling.

attacks.name: Chime
attacks.text: Search your opponent's discard pile for a Supporter card and use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack. (The Supporter card remains in your opponent's discard pile.)

flavorText: There is an orb inside its mouth. When it hops, the orb bounces all over and makes a ringing sound.

name: Shellos West Sea

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard a Water Energy attached to Shellos West Sea and remove all damage counters from Shellos West Sea.

flavorText: Its colors and shapes differ from region to region. In the Sinnoh region, two types are confirmed.

name: Aipom

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It lives high among the treetops. It can us its tail as freely and cleverly as its hands.

name: Mudsdale

attacks.name: Heavy Slam
attacks.text: This attack does 30 less damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: Mud that hardens around a Mudsdale's legs sets harder than stone. It's so hard that it allows this Pokémon to scrap a truck with a single kick.

name: Blacephalon

attacks.name: Fireworks Bomb
attacks.text: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like. If your opponent has exactly 3 Prize cards remaining, put 12 damage counters on them instead.

flavorText: It slithers toward people. Then, without warning, it triggers the explosion of its own head. It's apparently one kind of Ultra Beast.

name: Shiinotic

attacks.name: Strength Sap
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sleep Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Forests where Shiinotic live are treacherous to enter at night. People confused by its strange lights can never find their way home again.

name: Rattata

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It searches for food all day. It gnaws on hard objects to wear down its fangs, which grow constantly during its lifetime.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Single Lunge
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Bisharp pursues prey in the company of a large group of Pawniard. Then Bisharp finishes off the prey.

name: Alakazam E4 LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Alakazam E4. Alakazam E4 LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Damage Switch
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon SP to another of your Pokémon SP. This power can't be used if Alakazam E4 is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Jolteon

attacks.name: Electrigun
attacks.text: You may discard a Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: If agitated, it uses electricity to straighten out its fur and launch it in small bunches.

name: Barboach
flavorText: Its slimy body is hard to grasp. In one region, it is said to have been born from hardened mud.

name: Stunky

attacks.name: Severe Gas
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It protects itself by spraying a noxious fluid from its rear. The stench lingers for 24 hours.

name: Taillow

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It has a gutsy spirit that makes it bravely take on tough foes. It flies in search of warm climates.

name: Shiftry ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Eyes
abilities.text: After your opponent's Pokémon uses a Poké-Power, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Target Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. If that Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage instead. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Garchomp

attacks.name: Quick Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Royal Blades
attacks.text: If you played Cynthia from your hand during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: The protuberances on its head serve as sensors. It can even detect distant prey.

name: Sliggoo

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon's mucous can dissolve anything. Toothless, it sprays mucous on its prey. Once they're nicely dissolved, it slurps them up.

name: Regice ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Iceberg Crush
attacks.text: If Regirock ex is in play, flip a coin. If heads, discard 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Hydreigon

abilities.name: Dark Aura
abilities.text: All Energy attached to this Pokémon are Darkness Energy instead of their usual type.

attacks.name: Berserker Blade
attacks.text: Does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This extremely rare Pokémon is a different color than usual. It is very hard to find.

name: Great Ball
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Hippowdon

attacks.name: Triple Smash
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Stones can get stuck in the ports on their bodies. Dwebble help dislodge such stones, so Hippowdon look after these Pokémon.

name: Hariyama ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Commanding Aura
abilities.text: As long as Hariyama ex is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't play any Stadium cards from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

attacks.name: Pivot Throw
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Hariyama ex by attacks is increased by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Dome Fossil Kabuto
rules: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Kabuto you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Jigglypuff.

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Lucky Stadium
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Once during each player's turn (before attacking), that player may flip a coin. If heads, that player draws a card.

name: Turtwig

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The shell on its back is made of soil. On a very healthy TURTWIG, the shell should feel moist.

name: Switch
rules: Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Whismur

attacks.name: Uproar
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Usually, its cries are like quiet murmurs.  If frightened, it shrieks at the same volume as a jet plane.

name: Winona
rules: Search your deck for up to 3 Colorless Pokémon and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Stantler

attacks.name: Mystifying Horns
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Big Charge
attacks.text: If you have any Mega Evolution Pokémon on your Bench, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where reality is distorted.

name: Enhanced Hammer
rules: Discard a Special Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Super Scoop Up
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

attacks.name: Growth Spurt
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your hand to Exeggcute.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Ho-Oh LEGEND
rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Ho-Oh LEGEND.

abilities.name: Sacred Rainbow
abilities.text: All Energy attached to Ho-Oh LEGEND are Fire Energy instead of their usual type.

attacks.name: Bright Wing
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to Ho-Oh LEGEND.

flavorText: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world's skies continuously on its magnificent, seven-colored wings.

name: Ho-Oh

attacks.name: Scorching Wing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Fire Energy attached to Ho-Oh.

flavorText: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world's skies continuously on its magnificent seven-colored wings.

name: Tentacool

abilities.name: Cowardice
abilities.text: At any time during your turn (before your attack), you may return Tentacool to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to Tentacool.) This power can't be used the turn you put Tentacool into play or if Tentacool is affected by a Special Condition.

flavorText: Drifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by its stinging acid.

name: Hitmontop

attacks.name: Triple Kick
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Gut Spin
attacks.text: If Tyrogue is anywhere under Hitmontop, you may switch Hitmontop with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It fights while spinning like a top. The centrifugal force boosts its destructive power by ten.

name: Pokémon Communication
rules: Reveal a Pokémon in your hand and put it on top of your deck. If you do, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Celesteela

attacks.name: Moon Raker
attacks.text: If the total of both players' remaining Prize cards is exactly 6, this attack can be used for Metal.

flavorText: One kind of Ultra Beast. Witnesses have seen it burn down a forest by expelling gas from its two arms.

name: Gengar Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Gengar-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Marowak

attacks.name: Bone Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Assault Boom
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: It throws bones at Mandibuzz to knock it down. It's thought that Marowak is trying to avenge its parent.

name: Claydol ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Type Shift
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Claydol ex's type is Fighting until the end of your turn.

attacks.name: Psychic Boom
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Crush
attacks.text: You may discard a Psychic Energy card attached to Claydol ex. If you do, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Ghetsis
rules: Your opponent reveals his or her hand and shuffles all Item cards found there into his or her deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of Item cards your opponent shuffled into his or her deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: White Kyurem-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: White Inferno
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

name: Rocket's Hitmonchan ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Strikes Back
abilities.text: If Rocket's Hitmonchan ex is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Rocket's Hitmonchan ex is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mach Punch
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: M Gardevoir-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Brilliant Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Fairy Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Baltoy
flavorText: It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

name: Erika's Maids
rules: Trade 2 of the other cards in your hand for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and/or Evolution cards with Erika in their names from your deck. Show those cards to your opponent, then put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Darkrai & Cresselia LEGEND
rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Darkrai & Cresselia LEGEND.
When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lost Crisis
attacks.text: Choose 2 Energy attached to Darkrai & Cresselia LEGEND and put them in the Lost Zone. If any of your opponent's Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from this attack, put that Pokémon and all cards attached to it in the Lost Zone instead of discarding it.

attacks.name: Moon's Invite
attacks.text: Move as many damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon as you like to any of your opponent's other Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Energy Switch
rules: Move a basic Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Groudon ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Mark of Antiquity
abilities.text: As long as Groudon ex is your Active Pokémon, each player's Kyogre ex and Rayquaza ex can't attack.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage is not affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Crushing Mantle
attacks.text: You may discard from your hand as many Energy cards as you like. If you do, this attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card you discarded.

name: Aerodactyl ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Primal Lock
abilities.text: As long as Aerodactyl ex is in play, your opponent can't play Pokémon Tool cards. Remove any Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent's Pokémon and put them into his or her discard pile.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Ninja Boy
rules: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and switch it with that Pokémon. (Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle the first Pokémon into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Giant Wave
attacks.text: Wartortle can't attack during your next turn.

name: Eevee

abilities.name: Energy Evolution
abilities.text: When you attach a basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon that is the same type as that Energy card and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Quick Draw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Possessing an unbalanced and unstable genetic makeup, it conceals many possible evolutions.

name: Floatzel GL LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Floatzel GL. Floatzel GL LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Water Rescue
abilities.text: Whenever any of your Water Pokémon (excluding any Floatzel GL) is Knocked Out by damage from your opponent's attack, you may put that Pokémon and all cards that were attached to it from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Energy Cyclone
attacks.text: Choose as many Energy cards from your hand as you like and show them to your opponent. This attack does 20 damage times the number of Energy cards you chose. Put those Energy cards on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Erika's Perfume
rules: Look at your opponent's hand. If he or she has any Basic Pokémon cards there, you may put any number of them onto your opponent's Bench (as long as there's room).

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Circle Circuit
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Pokémon on your Bench.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The plan for today is to do nothing. My awesome tail is all curled up, and my ears are tired, too.

name: Horsea

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Reverse Thrust
attacks.text: Switch Horsea with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

name: Kingdra

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Twister
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life forms can survive.

name: Espeon

abilities.name: Solar Revelation
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Psy Report
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

flavorText: Its fur is so sensitive, it can sense minute shifts in the air and predict the weather.

name: Energy Search
rules: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Snubbull

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Small Pokémon flee from its scary face. It is, however, considered by women to be cute.

name: Regirock ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mend
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Fighting Energy card and attach it to Regirock ex. If you do, remove 1 damage counter from Regirock ex.

attacks.name: Metal Crush
attacks.text: If Registeel ex is in play, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Cyclone Energy
rules: Cyclone Energy provides Colorless Energy. When you attach this card from your hand to your Active Pokémon, switch 1 of the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Benched Pokémon to switch.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Let's Eat Together
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from both Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It has small electric sacs on both its cheeks. If threatened, it looses electric charges from the sacs.

name: Master Ball
rules: Look at the top 7 cards from your deck. Choose a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from those cards, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Put the other 6 cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Flygon ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Psychic Protector
abilities.text: If Flygon ex is damaged by an opponent's attack, you may discard up to 4 cards from your hand. If you do, any damage done to Flygon ex is reduced by 10 for each card you discarded.

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Flygon ex can't use Slashing Strike during your next turn.

name: Gyarados ★ δ
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

attacks.name: Spiral Growth
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to Gyarados Star.

attacks.name: All-out Blast
attacks.text: Discard cards from the top of your deck until you have 1 card left. This attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.text: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

name: Double Dragon Energy
rules: This card can only be attached to Dragon Pokémon. This card provides every type of Energy, but provides only 2 Energy at a time, only while this card is attached to a Dragon Pokémon.
(If this card is attached to anything other than a Dragon Pokémon, discard this card.)

name: Shadow Triad
rules: Put a Team Plasma card from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Rocket's Mewtwo ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Darkness Switch
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card attached to Rocket's Mewtwo ex, and then switch all damage counters on Rocket's Mewtwo ex with those on the Defending Pokémon. (If an effect of this attack is prevented, this attack does nothing.)

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Toxapex
flavorText: To attack, this Pokémon sends toxic spikes flying at its enemies. Ones that come close get mown down by the claws on its feet.

name: Rhyhorn

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging Tackles.

name: Remoraid

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Numbing Water
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It squirts water forcefully from its mouth to shoot down flying prey.

name: Claydol

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Explosion
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 120 damage to itself.

flavorText: This mysterious Pokémon started life as an ancient clay figurine made over 20,000 years ago.

name: Arceus
rules: You may have as many of this card in your deck as you like.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 Energy attached to Arceus.

flavorText: It is described in mythology as the Pokémon that shaped the universe with its 1,000 arms.

name: Kyogre ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Mark of Antiquity
abilities.text: As long as Kyogre ex is your Active Pokémon, each player's Groudon ex and Rayquaza ex can't attack.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Super Tidal Wave
attacks.text: You may show your hand to your opponent. If you do, this attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card in your hand. After doing damage, shuffle the Energy cards back into your deck.

name: Noivern-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Distort
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Item cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Sonic Volume
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Special Energy cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Boomburst-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Raticate

attacks.name: Gnaw Through
attacks.text: Discard a Pokémon Tool card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Super Fang
attacks.text: Put damage counters on the Defending Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.

flavorText: With its long fangs, this surprisingly violent Pokémon can gnaw away even thick concrete with ease.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Dig Deep
attacks.text: Put an Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Tail Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Burrows at high speed in search of food. The tunnels it leaves are used as homes by Diglett.

name: Fighting Stadium
rules: The attacks of each Fighting Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) do 20 more damage to the Defending Pokémon-EX (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Super Scoop Up
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

name: Ho-Oh-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Purifying Fire
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon has any basic Fire Energy attached to it, you may heal 50 damage from it.

attacks.name: Elemental Feather
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon).

name: Umbreon

attacks.name: Shadow Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike during your next turn.

flavorText: When exposed to the moon's aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it gains a mysterious power.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Reheat
attacks.text: Discard up to 2 Energy cards from your hand. For each card you discarded, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It controls balls of fire. As it grows, its six tails split from their tips to make more tails.

name: Dragonite ex δ
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Deafen
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Trainer cards (except for Supporter cards) from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dragon Roar
attacks.text: Put 8 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. If that Pokémon would be Knocked Out by this attack, put any damage counters not necessary to Knock Out the Defending Pokémon on your opponent's Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Kingdra

ancientTrait.name: α Growth
ancientTrait.text: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon (except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card), you may attach 2 Energy cards.

attacks.name: Gather Strength
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 4 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Dragon Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Water Energy and a Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life-forms can survive.

name: Rhyperior V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Drill Run
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heavy Rock Artillery
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Heavy Rock Artillery.

name: Turtwig
flavorText: Made from soil, the shell on its back hardens when it drinks water. It lives along lakes.

name: Sigilyph-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Mirror Counter
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sonic Wing
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Intercept-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Umbreon

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Lunatic Sense
attacks.text: Turn 1 of your face-down Prize cards face up. If that Prize card is a Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage. (That Prize card remains up for the rest of the game.)

flavorText: When exposed to the moon's aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it's filled with a mysterious power.

name: Metagross ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Magnetic Redraw
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Metagross ex is your Active Pokémon, you may use this power. Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player draws 4 cards. This power can't be used if Metagross ex is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Scanblast
attacks.text: Does 70 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has the same name as the Defending Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: All damage by attacks to Kakuna is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Goodra

abilities.name: Hydration
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Water Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, heal 20 damage from it.

attacks.name: Soaking Horn
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It gets picked on because it's meek. But then, whoever teased it gets to feel the full force of its horns and a good swatting from its thick tail.

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: On nights with a full moon, Clefairy gather from all over and dance. Bathing in moonlight makes them float.

name: Hand Scope
rules: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Swampert ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Energy Recycle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for 3 Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. If you do, your turn ends. This power can't be used if Swampert ex is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Ultra Pump
attacks.text: You may discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage and does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Superior Energy Retrieval
rules: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can't discard 2 cards, you can't play this card.) Put 4 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand. (You can't choose a card you discarded with the effect of this card.)
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Item Finder
rules: Discard 2 of the other cards from your hand in order to put a Trainer card from your discard pile into your hand.

name: Ho-Oh LEGEND
rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Ho-Oh LEGEND.

abilities.name: Sacred Rainbow
abilities.text: All Energy attached to Ho-Oh LEGEND are Fire Energy instead of their usual type.

attacks.name: Bright Wing
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to Ho-Oh LEGEND.

flavorText: Legends claim this Pokémon flies the world's skies continuously on its magnificent, seven-colored wings.

name: Salandit
flavorText: The males will do whatever the females tell them. They give the females most of their food. Due to malnutrition, the males can't evolve.

attacks.name: Continuous Eggsplosion
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Energy Support
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Psychic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Kommo-o-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Adamantine Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ultra Uppercut-GX
attacks.text: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Cobalion-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Metal Symbol
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Metal Energy attached to it can't be affected by any Special Conditions. Remove any Special Conditions affecting those Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dueling Saber
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Iron Rule-GX
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, their Pokémon can't attack. (This includes Pokémon that come into play on that turn.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Manectric ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Disconnect
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Trainer cards (except for Supporter cards) from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Mega Shot
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy attached to Manectric ex and then choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Rhyhorn
flavorText: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging Tackles.

name: Dialga-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Overclock
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Timeless-GX
attacks.text: Take another turn after this one. (Skip the between-turns step.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Energy Pouch
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, put all basic Energy attached to that Pokémon into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Good Manners
rules: In order to play this card, you can't have any Basic Pokémon cards in your hand. Show your hand to your opponent, then search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Magnifier
rules: Attach Magnifier to 1 of your Pokémon. At the end of your turn, discard Magnifier. If the Pokémon Magnifier is attached to attacks, don't apply Resistance for that attack.

name: Pokémon Ranger
rules: Remove all effects of attacks on each player and his or her Pokémon.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Combee

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Combee.

flavorText: The trio is together from birth. It constantly gathers honey from flowers to please Vespiquen.

name: Mimikyu

attacks.name: Filch
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Copycat
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon used an attack that isn't a GX attack during their last turn, use it as this attack.

flavorText: Its actual appearance is unknown. A scholar who saw what was under its rag was overwhelmed by horror and died of the shock.

name: Whiscash

abilities.name: Submerge
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It makes its nest at the bottom of swamps. It will eat anything—if it is alive, Whiscash will eat it.

name: VS Seeker
rules: Search your discard pile for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

name: Voltorb
flavorText: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

name: Minccino

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: These Pokémon prefer a tidy habitat. They are always sweeping and dusting, using their tails as brooms.

name: Metagross ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Metal Reversal
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch. If you do, this attack does 40 damage to the new Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Extra Comet Punch
attacks.text: During your next turn, Extra Comet Punch does 50 damage plus 50 more damage.

name: Espeon ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Devo Flash
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Espeon ex from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may choose 1 Evolved Pokémon on your opponent's Bench, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from that Pokémon, and put it back into his or her hand.

attacks.name: Snap Tail
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Psyloop
attacks.text: Does 60 damage plus 30 more damage for each Trainer card your opponent has in play.

name: Houndour

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It conveys its feelings using different cries. It works in a pack to cleverly take down prey.

name: Kyogre

attacks.name: Destructive Tsunami
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 40 damage to each of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A mythical Pokémon said to have swelled the seas with rain and tidal waves. It battled with Groudon.

name: Darkrai & Cresselia LEGEND
rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Darkrai & Cresselia LEGEND.
When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lost Crisis
attacks.text: Choose 2 Energy attached to Darkrai & Cresselia LEGEND and put them in the Lost Zone. If any of your opponent's Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from this attack, put that Pokémon and all cards attached to it in the Lost Zone instead of discarding it.

attacks.name: Moon's Invite
attacks.text: Move as many damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon as you like to any of your opponent's other Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Thundurus-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Headlock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Voltage Rush
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

name: Max Potion
rules: Heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon. Then, discard all Energy attached to that Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Leafeon LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Leafeon. Leafeon LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Energy Forcing
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Leafeon is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Verdant Dance
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

name: Counter Catcher
rules: You can play this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.
Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Granbull

attacks.name: Startling Bark
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 3 random cards from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Tantrum
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It can make most any Pokémon run away simply by opening its mouth wide to reveal its big fangs.

name: Potion
rules: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if that Pokémon has only 1).

name: Koga
rules: If an attack from a Pokémon with Koga in its name does damage to a Defending Pokémon this turn, that Pokémon is then Poisoned.

name: Dedenne

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Pachi
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Crackle…buzz…Dedenne…?! Beep…

name: Potion
rules: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Kartana

attacks.name: Divine Paper
attacks.text: If your opponent has exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: One of the Ultra Beast life-forms, it was observed cutting down a gigantic steel tower with one stroke of its blade.

name: Flygon LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Flygon. Flygon LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Wind Erosion
abilities.text: As long as Flygon is your Active Pokémon, discard the top card from your opponent's deck between turns.

attacks.name: Extreme Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon LV.X. This attack does 150 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon).

name: Hooligans Jim & Cas
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 3 random cards from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals those cards and shuffles them into his or her deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Swellow

abilities.name: Drive Off
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: If its two tail feathers are standing at attention, it is proof of good health. It soars elegantly in the sky.

name: Coalossal V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Mewtwo ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attached it to Mewtwo Star.

attacks.name: Psychic Star
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolved Pokémon, discard all Energy attached to Mewtwo Star and this attack does 50 damage plus 50 more damage.

name: Aggron ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rend
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Metal Surge
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Wingull
flavorText: It makes its nest on steep sea cliffs. Riding updrafts, it soars to great heights.

name: Registeel ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Block Signal
attacks.text: If Regice ex is in play, flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Wide Laser
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Regirock V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Raging Hammer
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rocky Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

name: Ambipom

attacks.name: Double Hit
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Hand Fling
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.

flavorText: Split into two, the tails are so adept at handling and doing things, Ambipom rarely uses its hands.

name: New Pokédex
rules: Shuffle your deck. Then, look at up to 5 cards from the top of your deck and rearrange them as you like.

name: Eevee

abilities.name: Energy Evolution
abilities.text: When you attach a basic Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon that is the same type as that Energy card and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Quick Draw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Possessing an unbalanced and unstable genetic makeup, it conceals many possible evolutions.

name: Sandshrew

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: To protect itself from attackers, it curls up into a ball. It lives in arid regions with minimal rainfall.

name: Arceus
rules: You may have as many of this card in your deck as you like.

attacks.name: Fastwave
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It is told in mythology that this Pokémon was born before the universe even existed.

name: Warp Energy
rules: Warp Energy provides Colorless Energy. When you attach this card from your hand to your Active Pokémon, switch that Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Rocket's Moltres ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Lift
abilities.text: If Rocket's Moltres ex has any Darkness Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost for Rocket's Moltres ex is 0.

attacks.name: Fire Dance
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

name: N
rules: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player draws a card for each of his or his remaining Prize cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Shroomish

attacks.name: Toxic Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.

name: Lt. Surge's Treaty
rules: Your opponent chooses 1 of the following: everyone chooses 1 of his or her own Prizes and put it into his or her hand, or you draw a card.

name: Vibrava

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It generates ultrasonic waves by violently flapping its wings. After making its prey faint, it melts the prey with acid.

name: Ledyba

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Ledyba does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is so timid, it can't move if it isn't with a swarm of others. It conveys its feelings with scent.

name: Corphish

attacks.name: Sharp Pincers
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: Its hardy vitality enables it to adapt to any environment. Its pincers will never release prey.

name: Aerodactyl-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

name: Diggersby
flavorText: With power equal to an excavator, it can dig through dense bedrock. It's a huge help during tunnel construction.

name: Salamence ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Volcanic Flame
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards from your deck.

attacks.name: Hydro Wave
attacks.text: Discard all Water Energy attached to Salamence ex. This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Rocket's Scizor ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dual Armor
abilities.text: As long as Rocket's Scizor ex has any Metal Energy attached to it, Rocket's Scizor ex is both Darkness and Metal type.

attacks.name: Rotating Claws
attacks.text: You may discard an Energy card attached to Rocket's Scizor ex. If you do, search your discard pile for an Energy card (excluding the one you discarded) and attach it to Rocket's Scizor ex.

name: Rhydon

attacks.name: Dirty Work
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck. If you played Giovanni's Exile from your hand during this turn, discard the top 5 cards instead.

flavorText: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Vulpix

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: At the time of birth, it has just one tail. Its tail splits from the tip as it grows older.

name: Potion
rules: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if that Pokémon has only 1).

name: Honedge

attacks.name: Cut Down
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: If anyone dares to grab its hilt, it wraps a blue cloth around that person's arm and drains that person's life energy completely.

name: Gardevoir ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Feedback
attacks.text: Count the number of cards in your opponent's hand. Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psystorm
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the total amount of Energy attached to all Pokémon in play.

name: M Aerodactyl-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rock Drill Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Lugia

attacks.name: Linear Attack
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

name: Shuckle

abilities.name: Pot Shell
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Shuckle by your opponent's Pokémon that has any Special Energy cards attached to it.

attacks.name: Ferment Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. As long as the Defending Pokémon remains Poisoned by this attack, it can't use any Poké-Body.

flavorText: It stores berries in its shell. The berries eventually ferment to become delicious juices.

name: Palkia-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spatial Control
attacks.text: Move any number of Energy from your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pressure
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zero Vanish-GX
attacks.text: Shuffle all Energy from each of your opponent's Pokémon into their deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Lt. Surge's Secret Plan
rules: Put 1 card from your hand face down onto your Bench. (You can't play this card if your Bench is full.) Treat that card as a Basic Pokémon as long as it's face down. Flip the card if either player needs to know what it is in order to use an attack, a Pokémon Power, or a Trainer card. Flip the card if it ever uses an attack or Pokémon Power, evolves, retreats, is damaged by an attack, or is otherwise affected by an attack. At any time during your turn, you may flip the card over. When you flip that card over, if it isn't a Basic Pokémon, discard it and all cards attached to it.

name: Switch
rules: Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Gallade E4 LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Gallade E4. Gallade E4 LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Blade Storm
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Gallade E4 LV.X from your hand onto your Active Gallade E4, you may put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aimed Cut
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Palkia & Dialga LEGEND
rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Palkia & Dialga LEGEND.
When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sudden Delete
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Put that Pokémon and all cards attached to it back to your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Time Control
attacks.text: Discard all Metal Energy attached to Palkia & Dialga LEGEND. Add the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck to his or her Prize cards.

name: Passimian

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Displaying amazing teamwork, they follow the orders of their boss as they all help out in the search for their favorite berries.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is nocturnal in nature. If it spots something shiny, its eyes glitter brightly.

name: Energy Reset
rules: Put as many Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Pikachu ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

attacks.name: Thundershock
