attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Spring Back
attacks.text: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage.

name: N
rules: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player draws a card for each of his or her remaining Prize cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Focus Sash
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If the Fighting Pokémon this card is attached to has full HP and would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, that Pokémon is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead. Then, discard this card.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: This is an extremely rare Meowth card. Maybe good fortune will come to you if you hold this card!

name: Wobbuffet

abilities.name: Bide Barricade
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, each Pokémon in play, in each player's hand, and in each player's discard pile has no Abilities (except for Psychic Pokémon).

attacks.name: Psychic Assault
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When she's surrounded by cushions and dressers that I like, she looks just like a princess!

name: Rayquaza ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dragon Boost
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Rayquaza ex from your hand onto your Bench, you may move any number of basic Energy cards attached to your Pokémon to Rayquaza ex.

attacks.name: Spiral Blast
attacks.text: Does 20 damage for each basic Energy card attached to Rayquaza ex.

name: Stakataka-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Ultra Wall
abilities.text: Your Ultra Beasts take 10 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Assembly-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card you have taken. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Potion
rules: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if that Pokémon has only 1).

name: Pokémon Catcher
rules: Switch your opponent's Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Salazzle
flavorText: Salazzle lives deep in caves and forces the Salandit it has attracted with its pheromones to serve it.

name: Arceus
rules: You may have as many of this card in your deck as you like.

attacks.name: Ripple Swell
attacks.text: If you have 6 Arceus in play and each of them is a different type, search your deck for up to 6 basic Energy cards. Attach each of those Energy cards to a different Pokémon you have in play. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Sky Spear
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Remove all Energy cards attached to Arceus and put them in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: It is said to have emerged from an egg in a place where there was nothing, then shaped the world.

attacks.name: Poison Seed
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Mudbray
flavorText: Loads weighing up to 50 times as much as its own body weight pose no issue for this Pokémon. It's skilled at making use of mud.

name: Ultra Necrozma-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Photon Geyser
attacks.text: Discard all basic Psychic Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Sky-Scorching Light-GX
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if the total of both players' remaining Prize cards is 6 or less. Put 6 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Head Ringer Team Flare Hyper Gear
rules: Attach this Pokemon Tool to 1 of your opponent's Pokemon-EX that doesn't already have a Pokemon Tool attached to it.
The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to cost Colorless more.
When this card is removed from a Pokémon for any reason, put this card in its owner's discard pile.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Slakoth

attacks.name: Smack ‘n' Slack
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It spends nearly all its time in a day sprawled out. Just seeing it makes one drowsy.

name: M Venusaur-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crisis Vine
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

name: Team Plasma Badge
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Pokémon this card is attached to is a Team Plasma Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Lileep

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Lileep.

flavorText: It lived on the seafloor 100 million years ago and was reanimated scientifically.

name: Seel

attacks.name: Freezing Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A Pokémon that lives on icebergs. It swims in the sea using the point on its head to break up ice.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Wurmple

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Often targeted by bird Pokémon, it desperately resists by releasing poison from its tail spikes.

name: Yamask

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

name: Chansey

attacks.name: Bind Wound
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: The eggs laid by Chansey are rich in nutrients and a favorite food of many Pokémon.

name: Cinccino

abilities.name: Smooth Coat
abilities.text: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.

attacks.name: Echoed Voice
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Echoed Voice attack does 50 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

name: Minion of Team Rocket
rules: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and return it and all cards attached to it to his or her hand. If 1 or both of them are tails, your turn ends immediately (you can't attack this turn).

name: Porygon-Z LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Porygon-Z. Porygon-Z LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Mode Crash
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Porygon-Z LV.X from your hand onto your Active Porygon-Z, you may discard all of your opponent's Special Energy cards in play.

abilities.name: Decode
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for up to any 2 cards and shuffle your deck afterward. Then, put those cards on top of your deck in any order. This power can't be used if Porygon-Z is affected by a Special Condition.

name: Clefable

abilities.name: Moon Guidance
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Their ears are sensitive enough to hear a pin drop from over a mile away, so they're usually found in quiet places.

name: Pokémon Personality Test
rules: Put an Evolution card from your hand face down in front of you. Your opponent guesses whether it is a Pokémon card with Light in its name, a Pokémon card with Dark in its name, or neither one. Flip the card over. If your opponent guessed right, he or she draws 3 cards. If your opponent guessed wrong, you draw 3 cards. Either way, return the card to your hand.

name: Galarian Yamask

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A clay slab with cursed engravings took possession of a Yamask. The slab is said to be absorbing the Yamask's dark power.

name: Skitty

attacks.name: Heal Bell
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It can't stop itself from chasing moving things, and it runs in a circle, chasing its own tail.

name: Professor Juniper
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Zygarde

attacks.name: Core Enforcer
attacks.text: Discard a Darkness Energy and a Fairy Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This is Zygarde's form at times when it uses its overwhelming power to suppress those who endanger the ecosystem.

name: Lucario-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aura Strike
attacks.text: If this Pokémon evolved from Riolu during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Cantankerous Beatdown-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Feraligatr ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Overpowering Fang
abilities.text: As long as Feraligatr ex is your Active Pokémon, each player's Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex) can't use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

attacks.name: Tsunami
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Sore Spot
attacks.text: Does 70 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Dusknoir LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Dusknoir. Dusknoir LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Ectoplasm
abilities.text: If Dusknoir is your Active Pokémon and would be Knocked Out by damage from your opponent's attack, you may discard all cards attached to Dusknoir LV.X and put Dusknoir LV.X as a Stadium card into play instead of discarding it. This counts as Dusknoir being Knocked Out and your opponent takes a Prize card. As long as you have Dusknoir LV.X as a Stadium card in play, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon between turns. If another Stadium card comes into play or Dusknoir LV.X is discarded by the effects of any attacks, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, Trainer, or Supporter cards, return Dusknoir LV.X to your hand.

name: Flygon ex δ
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Sand Damage
abilities.text: As long as Flygon ex is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Benched Basic Pokémon between turns. You can't use more than 1 Sand Damage Poké-Body between turns.

attacks.name: Psychic Pulse
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Ralts
flavorText: It is highly attuned to the emotions of people and Pokémon. It hides if it senses hostility.

name: Lugia LEGEND
rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Lugia LEGEND.

abilities.name: Ocean Grow
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Lugia LEGEND into play, you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck and attach all Energy cards you find there to Lugia LEGEND. Discard the other cards.

attacks.name: Elemental Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy, Water Energy, and Lightning Energy attached to Lugia LEGEND.

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

name: Flygon

abilities.name: Sand Flap
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose either player. That player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 4 cards.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Known as "The Desert Spirit," this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

name: Alakazam ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic Select
attacks.text: Put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Skill Copy
attacks.text: Discard a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from your hand. Choose 1 of that card's attacks. Skill Copy copies this attack. This attack does nothing if Alakazam Star doesn't have the Energy necessary to use that attack. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Alakazam Star performs that attack.

name: Special Charge
rules: Shuffle 2 Special Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Glameow

attacks.name: Act Cute
attacks.text: Your opponent puts a card from his or her hand on the bottom of his or her deck.

flavorText: When it's happy, Glameow demonstrates beautiful movements of its tail, like a dancing ribbon.

name: Spinda

attacks.name: Teeter Punch
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its steps are staggering and unsteady, but Spinda thinks it's walking in a straight line.

name: Zigzagoon

attacks.name: Pull Out
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for any 1 card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck.

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It walks in zigzag fashion. It is good at finding items in the grass and even in the ground.

name: Lass
rules: You and your opponent show each other your hands, then shuffle all the Trainer cards from your hands into your decks.

name: Wurmple

attacks.name: Needling Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It loves to eat leaves. If it is attacked by a STARLY, it will defend itself with its spiked rear.

name: Nosepass

attacks.name: Draw Toward
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: It moves less than an inch a year, but when it's in a jam, it will spin and drill down into the ground in a split second.

name: Gallade-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Swift Lunge
attacks.text: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Piercing Prizes
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your remaining Prize cards.

name: Dashing Pouch
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to discards Energy for its Retreat Cost, put that Energy into your hand instead of the discard pile.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Professor Elm
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 7 cards. You can't play any more Trainer cards this turn.

name: Croagunk
flavorText: It rarely fights fairly, but that is strictly to ensure survival. It is popular as a mascot.

attacks.name: Assist
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Ninetales ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Intense Glare
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch. The new Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Ninetales ex.

name: Coalossal VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Eruption Shot
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is an Energy card, this attack does 90 more damage, and attach that card to this Pokémon.

name: Zigzagoon

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: A Pokémon with abundant curiosity. It shows an interest in everything, so it always zigzags.

name: Dawn Wings Necrozma-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Invasion
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Flash
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Moon's Eclipse-GX
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent. Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Sleep Seed
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Kabutops ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hydrocutter
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the amount of Energy attached to Kabutops ex. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads. You can't flip more than 3 coins in this way.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counter from Kabutops ex (remove 1 if there is only 1).

name: Blissey

abilities.name: Fresh Egg
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal 80 damage from your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 80 damage to itself.

flavorText: Even the most ferocious Pokémon become calm when they eat Blissey's egg, which is said to be filled with happiness.

name: Galarian Runerigus

attacks.name: Spreading Spite
attacks.text: For each damage counter on this Galarian Runerigus, put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Mad Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A powerful curse was woven into an ancient painting. After absorbing the spirit of a Yamask, the painting began to move.

name: Misty's Wrath
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. Choose 2 of those cards and put them into your hand. Discard the rest.

name: Druddigon

abilities.name: Rough Skin
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It races through narrow caves, using its sharp claws to catch prey. The skin on its face is harder than a rock.

name: Vigoroth

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Its heart beats at a tenfold tempo, so it cannot sit still for even a moment.

name: Lickitung

attacks.name: Lap Up
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its tongue is twice the length of its body. It can be moved like an arm for grabbing food and attacking.

name: Salamence ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dragon Lift
abilities.text: The Retreat Cost for each of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex and Baby Pokémon) is 0.

attacks.name: Flame Jet
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to Salamence ex.

name: Celebi ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

abilities.name: Time Travel
abilities.text: If Celebi Star would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, you may flip a coin. If heads, Celebi Star is not Knocked Out, discard all cards attached to Celebi Star, and put Celebi Star on the bottom of your deck.

attacks.name: Leaf Shade
attacks.text: Count the amount of Energy attached to Celebi Star. Put that many damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Flabébé

attacks.name: Wish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: When it finds a flower it likes, it dwells on that flower its whole life long. It floats in the wind's embrace with an untroubled heart.

name: Gulpin

attacks.name: Starvin'
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: When Mom gets in the kitchen, the happiest things happen. I can feel my mouth watering!

name: Shiftry ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Skill Hack
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand and choose a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card you find there. Choose 1 of that Pokémon's attacks. Skill Hack copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) (No matter what type that Pokémon is, Shiftry ex's type is still Darkness.) Shiftry ex performs that attack.

attacks.name: Dirge
attacks.text: Does 60 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has the same name as the Defending Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Team Plasma Ball
rules: Search your deck for a Team Plasma Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Farfetch'd

attacks.name: Hard Swing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It can't live without the stalk it holds. That's why it defends the stalk from attackers with its life.

name: Rocket's Scyther ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dual Armor
abilities.text: As long as Rocket's Scyther ex has any Grass Energy attached to it, Rocket's Scyther ex is both Grass and Darkness type.

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: After your attack, you may switch Rocket's Scyther ex with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: Rocket's Scyther ex can't use Slashing Strike during your next turn.

name: Sprout Tower
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
All damage done by Colorless Pokémon's attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Weedle

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Often found in forests, eating leaves. It has a sharp, venomous stinger on its head.

name: Trapinch

attacks.name: Nest Building
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its jaws are strong enough to crush rocks but so heavy that it can't get up if it flips over. Sandile seize those moments as their chance.

name: Pokémon Breeder
rules: Put a Stage 2 Evolution card from your hand on the matching Basic Pokémon. You can only play this card when you would be allowed to evolve that Pokémon anyway.

name: Revive
rules: Put a Basic Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Aerodactyl Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Aerodactyl-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Fossil Researcher
rules: Search your deck for up to 2 in any combination of Amaura or Tyrunt and put them onto your bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Steelix Spirit Link
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Steelix-EX.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Doduo

attacks.name: Gatling Peck
attacks.text: Flip 5 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with telepathic power.

name: Old Amber Aerodactyl
rules: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Aerodactyl you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Champions Festival
rules: Once during each player's turn, if that player has 6 Pokémon in play, he or she may heal 10 damage from each of his or her Pokémon.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Linoone

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Dash Attack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It charges prey at speeds over 60 mph. However, because it can only run straight, it often fails.

name: Arceus ◇
rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

abilities.name: First Law
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Trinity Star
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you have Grass, Water, and Lightning Pokémon on your Bench. Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It is told in mythology that this Pokémon was born before the universe even existed.

name: Floette

attacks.name: Aromatherapy
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When the flowers of a well-tended flower bed bloom, it appears and celebrates with an elegant dance.

name: Ditto

abilities.name: Transform
abilities.text: During your turn (before your attack), you may put a Basic Pokémon from your hand on top of this Pokémon. (This does not count as playing that Pokémon or evolving.) This Pokémon is now that Pokémon. (Any cards attached to this Pokémon, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.)

flavorText: It can reconstitute its entire cellular structure to change into what it sees, but it returns to normal when it relaxes.

name: Sharpedo ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 20 damage to the new Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

attacks.name: Darkness Blast
attacks.text: You may discard a Darkness Energy attached to Sharpedo ex. If you do, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage and discard 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: M Gallade-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Unwavering Blade
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Super Scoop Up
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

name: Recycle
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card from your discard pile on top of your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Gardevoir ex δ
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Imprison
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Gardevoir ex is your Active Pokémon, you may put an Imprison marker on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Any Pokémon that has any Imprison markers on it can't use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies. This power can't be used if Gardevoir ex is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Flame Ball
attacks.text: You may move a Fire Energy card attached to Gardevoir ex to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Switch
rules: Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: M Charizard-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crimson Dive
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

name: Team Rocket's Evil Deeds
rules: Look at your opponent's hand and choose a card there. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck. Then, your opponent may draw up to 2 cards.

name: Lickilicky

attacks.name: Dangerous Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads. If the first flip is tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Their saliva contains lots of components that can dissolve anything. The numbness caused by their lick does not dissipate.

name: Devoured Field
rules: The attacks of Darkness Pokémon and Dragon Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) do 10 more damage to the opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Hippowdon LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Hippowdon. Hippowdon LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Sand Reset
abilities.text: Once during a game on your turn (before your attack), each player shuffles all cards in play (excluding Pokémon and Supporter cards) into his or her deck. You can't use more than 1 Sand Reset Poké-Power each game.

attacks.name: Double Shoot
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fighting Energy attached to Hippowdon and choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Purugly

attacks.name: Nyan Press
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.

name: Cofagrigus

attacks.name: Spirit Juggling
attacks.text: Discard any number of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Grave robbers who mistake them for real coffins and get too close end up trapped inside their bodies.

name: Palkia & Dialga LEGEND
rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Palkia & Dialga LEGEND.
When this Pokémon has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sudden Delete
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Put that Pokémon and all cards attached to it back to your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Time Control
attacks.text: Discard all Metal Energy attached to Palkia & Dialga LEGEND. Add the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck to his or her Prize cards.

name: Bagon

attacks.name: Rock Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: With its steel-hard stone head, it headbutts indiscriminately. This is because of the stress it feels at being unable to fly.

name: Pewter City Gym
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Don't apply Resistance to any attacks made by Pokémon with Brock in their names.

name: Skarmory ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Metal Gravity
abilities.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon retreats and has 40 or more remaining HP, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon. You can't use more than 1 Metal Gravity Poké-Body each turn.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Teary Eyes
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Ledyba by attacks is reduced by 20.

attacks.name: Quick Turn
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Jamming Net Team Flare Hyper Gear
rules: Attach this Pokemon Tool to 1 of your opponent's Pokemon-EX that doesn't already have a Pokemon Tool attached to it.
The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 less damage to all Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
When this card is removed from a Pokémon for any reason, put this card in its owner's discard pile.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Raichu ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dazzle Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Mega Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy cards attached to Raichu ex.

name: Rhyperior

attacks.name: Hefty Cannon
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Break Ground
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: From holes in its palms, it fires out Geodude. Its carapace can withstand volcanic eruptions.

name: Dusk Mane Necrozma-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Meteor Tempest
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sun's Eclipse-GX
attacks.text: You can use this attack only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Binacle

attacks.name: Dual Chop
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: After two Binacle find a suitably sized rock, they adhere themselves to it and live together. They cooperate to gather food during high tide.

name: Amulet Coin
rules: Attach Amulet Coin to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.
If the Pokémon Amulet Coin is attached to is your Active Pokémon at the end of your turn, draw a card.

name: Lillipup

attacks.name: Work Up
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Because it doesn't yelp, it's extremely popular with Trainers who live in apartment buildings.

name: Switch
rules: Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Shuppet

attacks.name: Gossip
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It loves vengeful emotions and hangs in rows under the eaves of houses where vengeful people live.

name: Darkness Energy
rules: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect unless the Attacking Pokémon is Darkness or has Dark in its name. Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Arceus
rules: You may have as many of this card in your deck as you like.

attacks.name: Lightning Turn
attacks.text: Switch Arceus with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is described in mythology as the Pokémon that shaped the universe with its 1,000 arms.

name: Doublade

attacks.name: Tool Drop
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool card attached to all Pokémon.

flavorText: The complex attack patterns of its two swords are unstoppable, even for an opponent greatly accomplished at swordplay.

name: Broken Time-Space
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
Each player may evolve a Pokémon that he or she just played or evolved during that turn.

name: Cosmog
flavorText: Even though its helpless, gaseous body can be blown away by the slightest breeze, it doesn't seem to care.

name: Palkia

attacks.name: Wormhole
attacks.text: Switch Palkia with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its total control over the boundaries of space enable it to transport itself to faraway places or even other dimensions.

name: Double Full Heal
rules: Remove all Special Conditions from each of your Active Pokémon.

name: Clobbopus
flavorText: It's very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

name: Mudsdale

attacks.name: Double Impact
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 120 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Mud that hardens around a Mudsdale's legs sets harder than stone. It's so hard that it allows this Pokémon to scrap a truck with a single kick.

name: Slaking

abilities.name: Unobservant
abilities.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Crushing Blow
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: The world's laziest Pokémon. When it is lounging, it is actually saving energy for striking back.

attacks.name: Entagling Thread
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Shinx
rules: Rawst Berry: If Shinx is Burned, remove the Special Condition Burned from Shinx at the end of each player's turn.

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Shinx.

name: Jirachi

attacks.name: Wish
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The twinkle of the stars. The destiny of love. A miracle that grants wishes in the night sky.

name: Ho-Oh ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Golden Wing
abilities.text: If Ho-Oh ex would be Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, you may move up to 2 Energy attached to Ho-Oh ex to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Rainbow Burn
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 20 more damage for each type of basic Energy card attached to Ho-Oh ex.

name: Rocket's Sneasel ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 10 damage to the new Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

attacks.name: Dark Ring
attacks.text: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Darkness Pokémon in play.

name: Heatran LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Heatran. Heatran LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Heat Metal
abilities.text: Your opponent can't remove the Special Condition Burned by evolving or devolving his or her Burned Pokémon. (This also includes putting a Pokémon Level-Up card onto the Burned Pokémon.) Whenever your opponent flips a coin for the Special Condition Burned between turns, treat it as tails.

abilities.name: Heat Wave
abilities.text: Once at the end of your turn, if Heatran is on your Bench, you may use this power. If you discarded basic Energy cards attached to your Fire or Metal Active Pokémon by using that Pokémon's attack this turn, attach up to 2 of those Energy cards to that Pokémon.

name: Bouffalant

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Head Charge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt.

name: Delcatty

attacks.name: Energy Salon
attacks.text: Search your deck for 3 different types of basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It dislikes dirty places. It often searches for a comfortable place in which to groom itself.

name: Taillow

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It has a gutsy spirit that makes it bravely take on tough foes. It flies in search of warm climates.

name: Regice ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Crystal Body
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, except damage, done to Regice ex by the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Freeze Lock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't attach Energy cards from his or her hand to the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.

name: Plasma Energy
rules: This card provides Colorless Energy.

name: Galarian Sirfetch'd

attacks.name: Meteor Assault
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Meteor Assault again until it leaves the Active Spot.

flavorText: Only Farfetch'd that have survived many battles can attain this evolution. When this Pokémon's leek withers, it will retire from combat.

name: Lugia LEGEND
rules: Put this card from your hand onto your Bench only with the other half of Lugia LEGEND.

abilities.name: Ocean Grow
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Lugia LEGEND into play, you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck and attach all Energy cards you find there to Lugia LEGEND. Discard the other cards.

attacks.name: Elemental Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy, Water Energy, and Lightning Energy attached to Lugia LEGEND.

flavorText: It is said to be the guardian of the seas. It is rumored to have been seen on the night of a storm.

attacks.name: Poison Sludge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Doduo

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Doduo does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

name: Fairy Drop
rules: Heal 50 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has any Fairy Energy attached to it.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lies around all day, becoming active near dusk. At night, it wanders the city in search of loose change.

name: Misty's Wish
rules: Look at 1 of your Prize cards. Then, ask your opponent if you may switch that card with 1 of the cards in your hand. If your opponent accepts and if you have any other cards in your hand, switch those cards. If your opponent declines, return the Prize card face down and draw a card.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is nocturnal in nature. If it spots something shiny, its eyes glitter brightly.

name: Infernape E4 LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Infernape E4. Infernape E4 LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Intimidating Roar
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Infernape E4 is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to Infernape E4.

name: Swellow

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Swallow Dive
attacks.text: If this Pokémon used Agility during your last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: If its two tail feathers are standing at attention, it is proof of good health. It soars elegantly in the sky.

name: Lunala-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lunar Javelin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Psychic Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Requiem-GX
attacks.text: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Show Off
attacks.text: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Payback
attacks.text: If your opponent has only 1 Prize card left, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It loves to sneak up on people late at night, then startle them with its shrieklike cry.

name: Espurr

attacks.name: Twinkle
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: My number one. Exceptionally cute. A little princess.

name: Latias ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

attacks.name: Healing Light
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon (including Latias Star).

attacks.name: Shooting Star
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, discard all Energy cards attached to Latias Star and this attack does 50 damage plus 100 more damage.

name: Multi Energy
rules: Attach Multi Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Multi Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play.) Multi Energy provides Colorless Energy when attached to a Pokémon that already has Special Energy cards attached to it.

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It can't relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes of dirt with its wings.

name: Fighting Memory
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
The Silvally-GX this card is attached to is a Fighting Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Drifloon

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: Drifloon does 10 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: Because of the way it floats aimlessly, an old folktale calls it a "Signpost for Wandering Spirits."

name: Fairy Garden
rules: Each Pokémon that has any Fairy Energy attached to it (both yours and your opponent's) has no Retreat Cost.
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

name: Herdier

abilities.name: Treasure Hunt
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Its dense black fur grows continuously. The high cost of keeping its hard fur properly groomed make this a troublesome Pokémon to train.

name: Arceus
rules: You may have as many of this card in your deck as you like.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It is told in mythology that this Pokémon was born before the universe even existed.

name: Life Dew
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 1 fewer Prize card.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

name: Metal Energy
rules: Damage done by attacks to the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect if the Pokémon that Metal Energy is attached to isn't Metal. Metal Energy provides Metal Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Tornadus-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blow Through
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Power Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Latios-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fast Raid
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack on your first turn.

attacks.name: Light Pulse
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

name: Pokémon Trader
rules: Trade 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards in your hand for 1 of the Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from your deck. Show both cards to your opponent. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Super Scoop Up
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Bidoof

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

name: Spinda

attacks.name: Dish Out
attacks.text: Draw a card from the top and the bottom of your deck.

attacks.name: Synchro Punch
attacks.text: If any basic Energy card attached to Spinda is the same type as any Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: No two Spinda have the same pattern of spots. Its tottering step fouls the aim of foes.

name: Slakoth

attacks.name: Drowsy
attacks.text: Both Slakoth and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.

flavorText: It spends nearly all its time in a day sprawled out. Just seeing it makes one drowsy.

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Litwick

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

name: Aegislash

abilities.name: Royal Guard
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Shield Bash
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Apparently, it can detect the innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to become king.

name: Barbaracle

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Hand Press
attacks.text: If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Seven Binacle come together to form one Barbaracle. The Binacle that serves as the head gives orders to those serving as the limbs.

name: Walrein ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Chilling Breath
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Walrein ex from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this power. Your opponent can't play any Trainer cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Wreck
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 70 damage plus 20 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.

name: Full Heal
rules: Remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Nincada

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It grows underground, sensing its surroundings using antennae instead of its virtually blind eyes.

name: Galarian Slowbro V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Rapid-Fire Poison
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may make your opponent's Active Pokémon Poisoned.

attacks.name: Tripping Shot
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

name: Snorlax

attacks.name: Double Lariat
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When its belly is full, it becomes too lethargic to even lift a finger, so it is safe to bounce on its belly.

name: Castform

attacks.name: Weather Teller
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Stadium cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It changes its form depending on the weather. Changes in the temperature or humidity appear to affect its cellular structure.

name: Florges

abilities.name: Calming Aroma
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon's attacks costs Fairy less.

attacks.name: Wonder Shine
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: In times long past, governors of castles would invite Florges to create flower gardens to embellish the castle domains.

name: Felicity's Drawing
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.
Discard up to 2 cards from your hand. If you discard 1 card, draw 3 cards. If you discard 2 cards, draw 4 cards.

name: Switch
rules: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Heal Powder
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, your Active Pokémon is no longer Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned and remove 2 damage counters from it. If your Active Pokémon has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

name: Trapinch

attacks.name: Dig
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its jaws are strong enough to crush rocks but so heavy that it can't get up if it flips over. Sandile seize those moments as their chance.

name: Swablu
flavorText: It can't relax if it or its surroundings are not clean. It wipes of dirt with its wings.

name: Alph Lithograph
rules: LOOK AT ALL OF YOUR FACE DOWN PRIZE CARDS!

name: Klefki

abilities.name: Key of Secrets
abilities.text: Each of your Metal Pokémon's Resistance is now -40.

flavorText: It never lets go of a key that it likes, so people give it the keys to vaults and safes as a way to prevent crime.

name: Volcanion-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Steam Up
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Fire Energy card from your hand. If you do, during this turn, your Basic Fire Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Volcanic Heat
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

name: Regirock ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Healing Stone
abilities.text: At any time between turns, remove 1 damage counter from Regirock ex.

attacks.name: Tonnage
attacks.text: You may do 60 damage plus 20 more damage. If you do, Regirock ex does 30 damage to itself.

name: Silicobra

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.

name: Dodrio

attacks.name: Raging Pecks
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: When Doduo evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.

name: Tornadus

attacks.name: Energy Wheel
attacks.text: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hurricane
attacks.text: Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

name: Arceus
rules: You may have as many of this card in your deck as you like.

attacks.name: Break Ground
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to have emerged from an egg in a place where there was nothing, then shaped the world.

name: Persian

attacks.name: Screech
attacks.text: During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 60 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It has a violent temperament. It will attack anything that looks it in the eye. Its sharp claws inflict deep wounds.

name: Fossil Excavation Kit
rules: Put 2 in any combination of Helix Fossil Omanyte, Dome Fossil Kabuto, or Old Amber Aerodactyl cards from your discard pile into your hand.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Rock Guard
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).
You can't have more than 1 ACE SPEC card in your deck.

name: Skitty

attacks.name: Charm
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It shows its cute side by chasing its own tail until it gets dizzy.

name: Furfrou

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Historically, in the Kalos region, these Pokémon were the designated guardians of the king.

name: Luxray GL LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Luxray GL. Luxray GL LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Bright Look
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Luxray GL LV.X from your hand onto your Active Luxray GL, you may switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flash Impact
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your Pokémon, and don't apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches the Defending Pokémon with it.

attacks.name: Firebreathing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Wooper
flavorText: This Pokémon lives in cold water. It will leave the water to search for food when it gets cold outside.

name: Lugia ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Silver Sparkle
abilities.text: If Lugia ex is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Lugia ex is Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads, choose an Energy card attached to the Attacking Pokémon and return it to your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Elemental Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy, Water Energy, and Lightning Energy attached to Lugia ex.

name: Cyrus's Conspiracy
rules: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Search your deck for a Supporter card, a basic Energy card, and a Trainer card that has Team Galactic's Invention in its name, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Feebas

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Count and Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card for each of your opponent's Pokémon that isn't an Evolved Pokémon.

flavorText: It is a shabby and ugly Pokémon. However, it is very hardy and can survive on little water.

name: Bunnelby

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It has ears like shovels. Digging holes strengthens its ears so much that they can sever thick roots effortlessly.

name: Energy Restore
rules: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, put a basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand. If you don't have that many basic Energy cards in your discard pile, put all of them into your hand.

name: Rolycoly
flavorText: Most of its body has the same composition as coal. Fittingly, this Pokémon was first discovered in coal mines about 400 years ago.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Resistance Gym
rules: This card stays in play after being played. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play.
Each Pokémon's Resistance is reduced by 20. (If a Pokémon's Resistance is -30, it becomes -10.)

name: Necrozma

attacks.name: Barrier Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Special Laser
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It looks somehow pained as it rages around in search of light, which serves as its energy. It's apparently from another world.

name: Stoutland

attacks.name: Ferocious Bellow
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 50 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Intelligent, good-natured, and valiant, it's a trustworthy partner on rescue teams.

name: Lysandre's Trump Card
rules: Each player shuffles all cards in his or her discard pile into his or her deck (except for Lysandre's Trump Card).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Darkness Energy
rules: If the Pokémon Darkness Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Ignore this effect unless the Attacking Pokémon is Darkness or has Dark in its name. Darkness Energy provides Darkness Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Grapploct

attacks.name: Full Nelson
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Tentacle Buster
attacks.text: If this Pokémon used Full Nelson during your last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

name: M Blastoise-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hydro Bombard
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Bibarel

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Hypno Headbutt
attacks.text: You may do 30 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It busily makes its nest with stacks of branches and roots it has cut up with its sharp incisors.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

name: Rocket's Snorlax ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Healer
abilities.text: As long as Rocket's Snorlax ex has any Darkness Energy attached to it, remove 1 damage counter from Rocket's Snorlax ex between turns.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Collapse
attacks.text: Rocket's Snorlax ex is now Asleep.

name: Lunala-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Lunar Javelin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Psychic Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Requiem-GX
attacks.text: (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Snorunt

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Snowball
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: In the snow country, certain folklore says a house will prosper if a SNORUNT lives there.

name: Typhlosion ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ring of Fire
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned, and can't retreat until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Split Blast
attacks.text: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Typhlosion ex. If your opponent has more than 1 Defending Pokémon, you may do 50 damage to each of them instead.

name: Rainbow Energy
rules: Attach Rainbow Energy to 1 of your Pokémon. While in play, Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card when not in play.) When you attach this card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon.

name: Lampent

attacks.name: Haunt
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It arrives near the moment of death and steals spirit from the body.

name: Recall
rules: For your attack this turn, your Active Pokémon can use any attack from its Basic Pokémon card or any Evolution card attached to it. (You still have to pay for that attack's Energy cost.)

name: Blastoise ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Energy Rain
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Water Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. Put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. This power can't be used if Blastoise ex is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hyper Whirlpool
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, your opponent discards an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Eevee
flavorText: Current studies show it can evolve into an incredible eight different species of Pokémon.

name: Kingdra ex δ
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Extra Smoke
abilities.text: Any damage done to your Stage 2 Pokémon-ex by your opponent's attacks is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Energy Link
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Kingdra ex.

attacks.name: Protective Swirl
attacks.text: Kingdra ex has no Weakness during your opponent's next turn.

name: Latios ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

attacks.name: Miraculous Light
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter and all Special Conditions from Latios Star.

attacks.name: Shining Star
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon, discard all Energy cards attached to Latios Star and this attack does 50 damage plus 100 more damage.

name: Squirtle

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Aqua Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Water Energy attached to Squirtle. This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It shelters itself in its shell, then strikes back with spouts of water at every opportunity.

name: Meowstic

attacks.name: Ear Influence
attacks.text: Move as many damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon as you like to any of your opponent's other Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Intense silence. Two lamplights. Keep waiting for night to end.

name: Berry
rules: Attach Berry to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

At any time between turns, if there are at least 2 damage counters on the Pokémon Berry is attached to, you may remove 2 of them and discard Berry. At the start of each turn, if there are at least 2 damage counters on the Pokémon Berry is attached to, remove 2 of them and discard Berry.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Zen Blade
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Zen Blade during your next turn.

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

name: Gladion
rules: Look at your face-down Prize cards and put 1 of them into your hand. Then, shuffle this Gladion into your remaining Prize cards and put them back face down. If you didn't play this Gladion from your hand, it does nothing.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Machamp LV.X
rules: Put this card onto your Active Machamp. Machamp LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: No Guard
abilities.text: As long as Machamp is your Active Pokémon, each of Machamp's attacks does 60 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance) and any damage done to Machamp by your opponent's Pokémon is increased by 60 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Strong-Willed
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if Machamp would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, Machamp is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

name: Bagon

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: With its steel-hard stone head, it headbutts indiscriminately. This is because of the stress it feels at being unable to fly.

name: Scoop Up
rules: Choose 1 of your Pokémon in play and return its Basic Pokémon card to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that card.)

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: M Latios-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sonic Ace
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Flareon ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

abilities.name: Crimson Ray
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Flareon Star from your hand onto your Bench, you may use this power. Each Active Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) is now Burned.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Flareon Star.

name: Professor Juniper
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Full Heal Energy
rules: If you play this card from your hand, the Pokémon you attach it to is no longer affected by a Special Condition. Full Heal Energy provides Colorless energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card.)

name: Thundurus

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Disaster Volt
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. It flies around the Unova region firing off lightning bolts.

name: Raikou

abilities.name: Extreme Speed
abilities.text: Raikou's Retreat Cost is Colorless less for each Lightning Energy attached to Raikou.

attacks.name: Raging Thunder
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your Pokémon and don't apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon that races across the land while barking a cry that sounds like crashing thunder.

name: Rayquaza ★
rules: You can't have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

attacks.name: Spiral Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Holy Star
attacks.text: Discard all Energy cards attached to Rayquaza Star. This attack does 100 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon-ex. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Jaw Fossil
rules: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Tyrunt you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Holon Energy FF
rules: Holon Energy FF provides Colorless Energy.

If the Pokémon that Holon Energy FF is attached to also has a basic Fire Energy card attached to it, that Pokémon has no Weakness. If the Pokémon that Holon Energy FF is attached to also has a basic Fighting Energy card attached to it, damage done by that Pokémon's attack isn't affected by Resistance. Ignore these effects if Holon Energy FF is attached to Pokémon-ex.

name: Stantler

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Frighten Horn
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon isn't an Evolved Pokémon, that Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Staring at its antlers creates an odd sensation as if one were being drawn into their centers.

name: Helix Fossil Omanyte
rules: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Omanyte you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Zamazenta

attacks.name: Metal Armament
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Amazing Shield
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon VMAX.

flavorText: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it being known as the Fighting Master's Shield. It was feared and respected by all.

name: Mew

abilities.name: Psyscan
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may have your opponent reveal his or her hand.

attacks.name: Psychic Exchange
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

flavorText: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

name: Solgaleo-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Shining Mane
abilities.text: Your Pokémon in play have no Weakness.

attacks.name: Turbo Strike
attacks.text: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
